Yvonne Chiang: That probably means...there's no need?;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐抬頭返回) >> <<#EndSimple>> Yvonne Chiang: Let me, uh, start with a self introduction. Yvonne Chiang: I'm Yvonne Chiang, 25, living with my parents. In prep school, working part-time.;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐紙條) >> Yvonne Chiang: ...Our community is called Meihu, in Wanpei city but on the outer rim. Yvonne Chiang: Address is No. 5, Yihe 3rd Street, 6th floor. Thought: You don't need to go that far into details... <<#Simple(6)>> ->Ahem! Yvonne Chiang: Um... Am I saying anything wrong?;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐紙條抬頭) >> Yvonne Chiang: Ah! Yvonne Chiang: You don't need that much information, maybe... Yvonne Chiang: Sorry, I ramble when I'm nervous...;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐紙條抬頭返回) >> ->... Yvonne Chiang: I'm from a big family, like, my mother has six sisters and one brother. <<#EndSimple>> Yvonne Chiang: Oh, I'm in prep school because I failed the college admission exam. Now I'm giving it another try. Yvonne Chiang: Prep school, and part-time working. Yeah.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐轉筆) >> Yvonne Chiang: The thing is, my parents can't quite afford to have me studying full-time.;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐紙條返回) >> Yvonne Chiang: Especially now, when they have a big bill to pay. Yvonne Chiang: I need this opportunity to contribute some real income. Yvonne Chiang: But the upcoming exam is six weeks away. I can't make any long term commitment. Yvonne Chiang: They told me flop farms pay good money, and quick. So, here I am.;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐前傾) >> Yvonne Chiang: I, uh, have done my homework, finding out what contributors need to do. Yvonne Chiang: I thought younger candidates had a better chance?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐待機) >> <<#If($AskHelmetHowToPick = -1)>> Thought: Lawrence did say younger candidates are better... <<#EndIf>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yes. We do prefer younger candidates. ($HelmetSaidYoungGood) ;<>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐點頭) >> Yvonne Chiang: Really? Great!;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐前傾期待) >> Yvonne Chiang: Wow, that's perfect! Yvonne Chiang: I, um... Thank you. Yvonne Chiang: I really, really need this job.;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐前傾期待返回) >> ->No such thing. Yvonne Chiang: ...I see. Yvonne Chiang: I must have got it wrong. Sorry. Yvonne Chiang: Let me continue with my introduction. ->Sorry, I can't disclose confidential information. Yvonne Chiang: Ah, sorry! Yvonne Chiang: Shouldn't have asked that. I apologize! <<#Simple(6)>> ->No biggie. Relax. Yvonne Chiang: O-OK! ->Please do your homework better next time. Yvonne Chiang: Yes, sir... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Honestly, I'm not sure. Yvonne Chiang: Ah... Yvonne Chiang: I see... <<#EndSimple>> Yvonne Chiang: Um... Where was I again?;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐前傾返回) >> Yvonne Chiang: I'm the only child. My parents try to provide me with everything they can manage.;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐抬頭) >> Yvonne Chiang: Although we are not very rich... Yeah.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐轉筆) >> Yvonne Chiang: Still, they sent me to an expensive school. Yvonne Chiang: ...Doing everything to make sure I could have a decent future. Yvonne Chiang: And I want to make them proud, by getting into a great college.;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐抬頭回想) >> Yvonne Chiang: But I failed three times in a row. This year would be my fourth try. Yvonne Chiang: I haven't made a single penny this whole time, and our restaurant isn't doing very well... Yvonne Chiang: There used to be quite a few restaurants on our street. Now we're about the last one standing.;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐抬頭回想返回) >> Yvonne Chiang: This is unfair. I mean, ramen is the best when made the traditional way, won't you say so? Yvonne Chiang: But these days it's all about franchised stores, doing everything with machines...;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐抬頭) >> Yvonne Chiang: Quick order, quick service. And cheaper, too. Yvonne Chiang: Cleaning bots keep the restaurant sparkling all the time... Thought: Taking your time with a traditional bowl of hand-made ramen is a true luxury these days.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐抬頭返回) >> Yvonne Chiang: ...It wasn't like this before. When I was a little girl, the city was full of independent shops and restaurants. Yvonne Chiang: Now big companies are taking over everything with their franchised stores. Yvonne Chiang: I guess you don't see many mom & pop stores where you live, sir?;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐抬頭抬手) >> Thought: Plenty of them, in fact. Dingan is what you'd call a "traditional community". <<#Simple(99999)>> ->That is unrelated to our session. Yvonne Chiang: Ah, right...;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐抬頭抬手返回) >> Yvonne Chiang: Sorry for wandering off. ->Actually, we have quite a few of them. Yvonne Chiang: Wow, I'm surprised.;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐抬頭抬手返回) >> Yvonne Chiang: You live in one of the traditional communities? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->This is a bit off topic. <<#GoTo(@啊蔣瑜涵又偏題嚕)>> ->Yep. Good guess. Yvonne Chiang: Good for you! Yvonne Chiang: Plenty of nice surprises in those tiny shops!;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐抬頭回想) >> Yvonne Chiang: Is your community planned for elevation? Yvonne Chiang: I'd like to live in such a community for a change.;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐抬頭回想返回) >> <<@啊蔣瑜涵又偏題嚕>> Yvonne Chiang: Ah! Yvonne Chiang: Sorry, I'm blabbering again! <<#EndSimple>> ->I'm not very inclined to talk about my privacy. Yvonne Chiang: S-sorry! I didn't mean to be nosy.;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐抬頭抬手返回) >> <<#EndSimple>> Yvonne Chiang: Anyhow... Yvonne Chiang: Since I'm preparing for the exam, full time jobs are out of the question.;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐抬頭後傾) >> Yvonne Chiang: ... Yvonne Chiang: I really, really have to get an admission this time. Yvonne Chiang: A great college, the exact major doesn't matter.;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐抬頭後傾返回) >> Yvonne Chiang: My parents have been working so hard to pay my tuition. Yvonne Chiang: They believe in me, told me to focus on my studies and not worry about money. Yvonne Chiang: I'm very grateful.;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐抬頭返回) >> Yvonne Chiang: Yet every time they meet up with my uncles and aunties... Yvonne Chiang: They come home mean-ish. Yvonne Chiang: They talk about how someone else's child got into a great college... Yvonne Chiang: ...And ask why can't I do the same. Yvonne Chiang: ...;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐思考) >> Yvonne Chiang: So, I can't fail again. Not this time. Yvonne Chiang: I can't live with myself, leeching off them like that. Yvonne Chiang: Life is tough enough for them, even without me... Yvonne Chiang: Showing them a college admission is the least I could do...;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐思考返回) >> Yvonne Chiang: I know it's not my place to ask, but in this case I have to... Yvonne Chiang: Because I'm a bad daughter, a burden... Yvonne Chiang: I must, have to, seize this opportunity and show them my worth. Yvonne Chiang: So, could you please select me as a contributor?;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐前傾) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sorry, I can't do that.;<>;//有承諾不管是錄取還不錄取都一樣 Yvonne Chiang: ... Yvonne Chiang: ... Yvonne Chiang: ...;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐前傾返回) >> Yvonne Chiang: I see. <<#GoTo(@抱歉瑜涵我不會錄取你欸)>> ->Sure. You are selected.;<> Yvonne Chiang: That is GREAT!;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐前傾期待) >> Yvonne Chiang: I can't thank you enough! Yvonne Chiang: You have no idea how much this means to me! Yvonne Chiang: You just saved my life, sir! Yvonne Chiang: That's it?;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐前傾期待返回) >> Yvonne Chiang: I'm going home to let them know! Yvonne Chiang: Have a nice day, sir! Thought: Now I have a promise to keep.;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐前傾返回) >> <<#End>> ->I can't promise anything yet.;<> Yvonne Chiang: I understand... <<#EndSimple>> Yvonne Chiang: So... Yvonne Chiang: As long as this session goes, would you say I have a chance? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Definitely! You have a chance.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐點頭) >> Yvonne Chiang: Good! That is GREAT!;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐前傾期待) >> Yvonne Chiang: Thank you! Thank you so much! Yvonne Chiang: Great... Yvonne Chiang: I'll be going then. See you!;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐前傾期待返回) >> <<#End>> ->Like I said, I can't promise anything yet. ->You're only the second candidate I've seen today. Yvonne Chiang: Ah, I see.;<< Play (蔣瑜涵, MBU_蔣瑜涵_側坐前傾返回) >> <<#EndSimple>> <<@抱歉瑜涵我不會錄取你欸>> Yvonne Chiang: Thank you. Yvonne Chiang: I'll be going then. <<#End>>

title: 面談_對象董欣麗
body: <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Hello. ->Let's begin. <<#EndSimple>> Sydney Tung: Hello. My legal name is Sydney Tung. Thought: This is Sydney Tung? Thought: The profile says she's 23 years old! Sydney Tung: Hmmm... Sydney Tung: ... Thought: Why doesn't she start? Thought: That gaze, as if suggesting something...? <<#Simple(6)>> ->You may start with a self introduction. Sydney Tung: Oh, right... Yes, introduction.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐低頭) >> Thought: Shifty eyes. Dodgy. Thought: What is she planning? <<#GoTo(@我退休前是個公務員，現在靠政府給的退休金生活。)>> ->I'm sorry, but your age doesn't seem to qualify as a candidate. Sydney Tung: Y-yes. You're right.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐低頭) >> Sydney Tung: Still, let's continue with the session, shall we? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Sydney Tung: L-let me start with a self introduction. Won't want to keep you occupied for too long. <<@我退休前是個公務員，現在靠政府給的退休金生活。>> Sydney Tung: I was a public servant, now retired. I have a pension plan.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐低頭返回) >> Sydney Tung: Although...it's not much. You must have seen how the government keeps cutting back retirement pension plans...;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐轉筆) >> Sydney Tung: That is why I need this opportunity. Sydney Tung: I know that you prefer younger candidates.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐抬手) >> Sydney Tung: And someone not as fragile as I am... Sydney Tung: But I believe I meet your minimum requirement, at least... Thought: No way. This granny has no chance... Sydney Tung: ...;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐抬手返回) >> Sydney Tung: Sorry... Sydney Tung: I know I'm well past the appropriate age... Sydney Tung: Honestly, I'm 68 now.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐放胸) >> Sydney Tung: Had to ask a friend for help, to pass the initial screening... Thought: Meaning I shouldn't trust her medical report... Sydney Tung: This session may be a waste of your time. Sydney Tung: I certainly wouldn't want that. Sydney Tung: ...Kindly let me know, if you think I should show myself out.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐放胸返回) >> Sydney Tung: What do you think?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐待機) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You shouldn't be here. Sydney Tung: I will show myself out, then.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐點頭) >> Sydney Tung: Sorry for wasting your time. <>;<<#End>> ->No, please stay. Sydney Tung: Really? I thought I had no chance, being this old. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Can't say for sure yet. Let's continue this session. Sydney Tung: I appreciate, really.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐點頭) >> ->I need you to tell me how you slipped through the screening process. <<#GoTo(@說服董欣麗講清楚她是怎麼跑進來的)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<@董小姐回到面談裡啦啦啦>> Sydney Tung: For a kind soul like you, I should be honest.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐後傾) >> Sydney Tung: Truth is, I'm not very healthy.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐轉筆) >> Sydney Tung: ...That is to be expected, I suppose, given my age. Sydney Tung: A friend pulled strings to get me through the medical screening. Sydney Tung: I know it's unfair to other candidates, but I really need the money.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐後傾返回) >> Sydney Tung: The money is not for enjoyment or paying bills. My pension covers that. Sydney Tung: I need the money to buy a ticket to the South, out of my own pocket. Thought: Paying out of pocket? A ticket out of Wanpei costs a lot without approval from the government...;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐低頭) >> Sydney Tung: I have been saving up for months, but it's far from enough...;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐低頭) >> Sydney Tung: And trust me, I tried, again and again, to apply for a travel permit and ticket subsidy. Sydney Tung: They just refuse to approve...;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐低頭返回) >> Sydney Tung: Maybe the Travel Authority doesn't think old women like me have any legitimate reason to leave Wanpei. Sydney Tung: But I've been here, all on my own, for too long.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐低頭返回) >> Sydney Tung: Nowadays I'd give everything just to see my son and his family.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐前傾) >> Sydney Tung: He left Wanpei and found a career in the South many, many years ago. Sydney Tung: I didn't mind back then, because my husband was here. We had each other. Sydney Tung: Plus, we could see our boy on video calls. Sydney Tung: Things changed earlier this year, when my husband was gone. It's like...time has suddenly slowed down.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐前傾返回) >> Sydney Tung: ...;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐低頭) >> Sydney Tung: I guess I just don't want to be lonely. Sydney Tung: I know there are other candidates, and many of them need this opportunity for a living... Sydney Tung: If I were you, I'd prioritize them, right?;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐低頭返回) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐待機) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yes. This session is a waste of time, both yours and mine.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐點頭) >> Sydney Tung: But you just said... Sydney Tung: Good. I see.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐愧疚) >> Sydney Tung: Let's end the session here. Sorry for wasting your time. Thought: ... Thought: What have I done! She looks heartbroken... <>;<<#End>> ->I won't call your need any less legitimate. Sydney Tung: Thank you... Thank you for saying that. ->Yes. There are people who need the money more than you do.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐點頭) >> Sydney Tung: I see what you mean. Sydney Tung: Let's wrap up this session, then.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐抬手) >> Sydney Tung: There is no point, and I don't want to force you into making hard decisions. Sydney Tung: I will show myself out, then.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐抬手返回) >> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Wait, you still have a chance. Sydney Tung: Do I? I thought you were suggesting... Sydney Tung: Thank you for giving me a second chance. <<#GoTo(@董欣麗說說來也好笑。)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Sydney Tung: And thank you for talking to me. Having someone to talk to feels wonderful.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐愧疚) >> Sydney Tung: …… Sydney Tung: Maybe that is what I'm looking for, really, to find someone to talk to. Sydney Tung: This is a pleasant conversation. Have a nice day. <>;<<#End>> <<#EndSimple>> <<@董欣麗說說來也好笑。>> Sydney Tung: Funny, come to think about it...;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐愧疚) >> Sydney Tung: I'm what they call an independent woman, all the time.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐愧疚抬頭) >> Sydney Tung: Supported the whole family all on my own. Sydney Tung: My husband never helped much, other than making a mess for me to clean up.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐愧疚抬頭返回) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐低頭) >> Sydney Tung: Didn't make much money, didn't help around the house. Sydney Tung: My son isn't any better. Didn't even want to stay at home. Sydney Tung: Left us to find his fortune in Wanchung. Started his own family there. Sydney Tung: Now I have a grandson, who only exists on the screen. Video calls. Sydney Tung: I have never kissed or hugged him yet.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐愧疚返回) >> Sydney Tung: Call me selfish if you want.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐待機) >> Sydney Tung: I really want to hug him for once, before the end of my time.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐摀嘴) >> Sydney Tung: To hold those chubby hands. Sydney Tung: I know I'm privileged, compared to other candidates. Sydney Tung: A home in the city. A pension. What else could I ask for? Sydney Tung: Yet here I am, asking for even more. Sydney Tung: I am a greedy old woman and I'm aware of it...;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐摀嘴返回) >> <<#Simple(12)>> ->Life isn't all happiness. Maybe you should come to terms with it. Sydney Tung: I tried, young man, I did.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐放胸) >> Sydney Tung: But every day, when the sun starts to set and there is only me in a deserted house... Sydney Tung: The loneliness feels crushing... Sydney Tung: Sorry, I guess that was more than you'd like to hear.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐放胸返回) >> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Don't say that. Sydney Tung: Thank you. You're a considerate, good man. <<#GoTo(@我也知道你一直在顧慮我的心情。董欣麗這麼說)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Sydney Tung: I suppose I have occupied you long enough.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐後傾) >> Sydney Tung: Time to leave. I will wait for your notice. Sydney Tung: With everything said, I really appreciate if I could be selected. Sydney Tung: This could be my last trip. Sydney Tung: Thank you for spending so much time with me.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐後傾返回) >> Sydney Tung: Hopefully I will hear from you again. <>;<<#End>> ->I wouldn't call you greedy. Sydney Tung: You're a kind soul, mister. Sydney Tung: Let's call a spade a spade. I am selfish. ->You're a hypocrite. Sydney Tung: I, um... <<#Simple(6)>> ->All this talk about feeling bad for being greedy, yet you're not leaving. Sydney Tung: Didn't you ask me to stay? <<#Simple(6)>> ->I did, but you could have said no. Sydney Tung: ... <<#Simple(6)>> ->You didn't. Instead, you were counting on me to make moral decisions for you. Sydney Tung: I didn't mean to... Sydney Tung: I-I didn't realize that... Sydney Tung: I'm so, so sorry.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐低頭) >> <<#GoTo(@超咄咄逼人把董欣麗趕走)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Sydney Tung: I see... <<@超咄咄逼人把董欣麗趕走>> Sydney Tung: Let's end the session here. Sorry for wasting your time. Thought: ... Thought: What have I done! She looks heartbroken... <>;<<#End>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<@我也知道你一直在顧慮我的心情。董欣麗這麼說>> Sydney Tung: I see that you care for my feelings. Sydney Tung: However, we both know I'm probably the least qualified of all candidates.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐後傾) >> Sydney Tung: Physically not fit. I admit. Sydney Tung: And this may have a negative impact on your performance review. Sydney Tung: Still, I'm not ready to simply give up... Sydney Tung: Please consider me if it doesn't cause you any trouble.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐後傾返回) >> Sydney Tung: I appreciate, really. Sydney Tung: Goodbye. Thought: ... <>;<<#End>>

title: 說服董欣麗供出誰放她進來的
body:
<<@說服董欣麗講清楚她是怎麼跑進來的>>
<<Set($AskDongXinLiHowSheIn,true)>>;
Sydney Tung: I, um...
Sydney Tung: Sorry, I can't tell you.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐低頭) >>
Sydney Tung: How can I backstab someone who helped me?
<<#Simple(10)>>
->Is it an insider? Someone who works in this farm?
    Sydney Tung: N-no.
    Thought: She's panicking.
->Why do you call it backstabbing?
    Sydney Tung: Because you may have him fired.
    Thought: Fired, huh. So it must be an insider job.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
 
Sydney Tung: I can't say any more than that.
<<#Simple(10)>>
->How exactly did he have you pass the medical screening?
    Sydney Tung: That I don't know. He has his ways.
->How did you pass the medical screening?
    Sydney Tung: As I told you, there was a friend.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Sydney Tung: Please don't ask any further.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐低頭返回) >>
 
<<#Simple(10)>>
->Have you ever thought you can't possibly slip through this in-person consultation?
    Sydney Tung: I had my concerns, but he said it's not a problem.
    Sydney Tung: So I was expecting...;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐抬手) >>
    <<#Simple(6)>>
    ->Expecting what?
        Sydney Tung: I thought this session wouldn't go like this.
        Thought: Hmmm...
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Sydney Tung: No, I can't talk any more about it.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐抬手返回) >>
->What's with the anxiety when you first came in here?
    Sydney Tung: An-anxiety?
    Sydney Tung: You mean...?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->You were kind of...eying me in a weird way.
    ->I think you were trying to suggest something?
        Sydney Tung: I just thought you were someone else.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐低頭) >>
        Sydney Tung: Thought you were one of my...
        Sydney Tung: ...my friends.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐低頭返回) >>
        Thought: Apparently I'm not supposed to be conducting this consultation.
        Thought: This friend of hers planned to host this session and let her pass.;<<Set($WellPlayedFoundInterviewClue,0)>>
    <<#EndSimple>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
 
Sydney Tung: Let's get back to the session.
Sydney Tung: Shall we...?
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->No. I can't let this session go on.
    Sydney Tung: I see...;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐愧疚) >>
    Sydney Tung: Thought so...
    <<#Simple(6)>>
    ->Not unless you let me know who is your helper.
        Sydney Tung: ...
        Sydney Tung: He helped me so much, or else I wouldn't be sitting here today.
        Sydney Tung: He did it on his own risk.
        Sydney Tung: So, sorry, I won't betray him.;<< Play (董欣麗, MBU_董欣麗_側坐愧疚抬頭) >>
        Thought: She looks nervous, but firm.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Sydney Tung: I won't waste any more of your time, then.
    Sydney Tung: I will show myself out, then.
    Sydney Tung: Really sorry.
    <<Set($EndingOfDongXinLi,3)>>;<<#End>>
->Yeah, let's continue.
    <<#GoTo(@董小姐回到面談裡啦啦啦)>>
<<#EndSimple>>

title: 面談_對象劉漢銘
body: <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Hello. ->Let's begin. <<#EndSimple>> Hank Liu: Good day, my name is Hank Liu. Hank Liu: Nice to meet you... Thought: ...!? Hank Liu: Eh!?;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐摸嘴) >> Hank Liu: You're...! Thought: He's Fran's neighbor... Thought: Totally not expecting to meet him again, not here. //public float FinalCheckNoiseEnd = 0; //public float FinalCheckBotherEnd = 0; Hank Liu: Oh hi, Mr. Dai. Wasn't expecting to see you here.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐摸嘴返回) >> <<#If($FinalCheckBotherEnd >-1)>> Hank Liu: Um... Thought: Our previous conversation didn't end well...;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐抬頭) >> <<#If($FinalCheckBotherEnd > 1)>> Thought: He's got this shitty attitude. Thought: In the end he apologized, though, which counts... <<#EndIf>> Hank Liu: Before we start, let's make this clear...;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐抬手) >> Hank Liu: Our previous, uh, disagreement, doesn't have any effect on this session, does it?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐抬頭返回) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Honestly, I think it does. Hank Liu: ...;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐轉筆) >> Hank Liu: Excuse me?;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐抬手返回) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Meaning I won't let you pass. Don't even think about it. Hank Liu: That... Hank Liu: Could you please reconsider? <<#GoTo(@對於稍早發生的事情，完全是我的錯。)>> ->Meaning our little disagreement will count toward my final decision. Hank Liu: ...;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐低頭) >> Hank Liu: Okay, won't blame you. Thought: That's it? The Hank I know wouldn't admit defeat without a fight. Hank Liu: Then, please allow me to apologize.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐低頭返回) >> <<@對於稍早發生的事情，完全是我的錯。>> Hank Liu: What happened back then, it's all my fault.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐道歉) >> Hank Liu: I shouldn't have judged you on your private life. Hank Liu: Yeah. My fault. I'm sorry. Hank Liu: Can we make peace, please? Thought: He's like a totally different person... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Apology accepted. Hank Liu: Thank you.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐道歉返回) >> Hank Liu: Let's get on with the session. <<#GoTo(@之前不愉快事件結束)>> ->You're only saying that for this opportunity. I wasn't born yesterday, you know. <<@你要我劉漢銘滾>> Hank Liu: ...;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐道歉返回) >> Hank Liu: Okay. You want me to fuck off?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐待機) >> Hank Liu: I'll make myself scarce. You happy now?;<< PlayUntil (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐生氣) >> Hank Liu: Before I do, though, I'd let you know that you're one fucked up bastard!;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐生氣手指) >> Hank Liu: Someone is going to die because you screwed up your damn job! Thought: ... <>;<<#End>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->Of course not. Personal grudge has nothing to do with my work. Hank Liu: Great, then...;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐抬手返回) >> Thought: He looks massively relieved. Hank Liu: I'll start, then. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> <<#If($FinalCheckBotherEnd=-1)>> Hank Liu: Small world, isn't it? Thought: Glad our previous conversation didn't develop into a full-on fight. Or else it would be very awkward now. Hank Liu: So... Hank Liu: I'll start, if you're OK with it?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐待機) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah, please begin. Hank Liu: Sure.;<< PlayUntil (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐點頭) >> ->Before anything, may I ask why you are here today? Hank Liu: ... Thought: Can't read his face. Sort of complicated. Hank Liu: We will come to that soon enough. Hank Liu: I'll start from the beginning. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> <<@之前不愉快事件結束>> Hank Liu: My name is Hank Liu, 59, work for Vision, construction supervisor. Hank Liu: ...;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐摸嘴) >> Hank Liu: I mean, used to work for Vision. Was a construction supervisor back then.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐抬頭) >> Hank Liu: Nowadays? Unemployed. Thought: I see why he's applying for this contributor opportunity now.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐抬頭返回) >> Hank Liu: Career-wise, it's pretty simple. My dad was a construction worker. I followed suit.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐摸嘴返回) >> Hank Liu: Worked for thirty plus years. Vision had this new construction project the year before last, and they gave me a promotion. Supervisor.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐轉筆) >> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why were you fired? Hank Liu: Woah, don't make assumptions. Wasn't my fault. Hank Liu: Last month them assholes high up in the hierarchy started to play with this AI management system.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐手指) >> Hank Liu: Said it's good for efficiency. What do I know, right? Hank Liu: I say they're only looking for an excuse to get rid of us old boys! Hank Liu: How smart are their machines, huh? Better than my thirty years of experience?;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐手指返回) >> Thought: AI is capable of construction supervision these days? Thought: So, what? Machines are ordering human workers around now? Hank Liu: Hey, let's not talk about that, alright? Irrelevant. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm curious how could AI replace you. Hank Liu: ... Hank Liu: Should have seen that coming, to be honest with you. <<#GoTo(@本來現在真正在施工的大多都已經是機器了。)>> ->Irrelevant or not, I'm the judge of that. Hank Liu: ... Hank Liu: Okay, go on with your questions. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm out of questions.;<> Hank Liu: ... Thought: He's trying to hold down the rage. He thinks I'm taunting. Hank Liu: I'll continue, then. ->You said Vision has AI for construction supervision now? Hank Liu: Yes.;<< PlayUntil (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐點頭) >> <<@本來現在真正在施工的大多都已經是機器了。>> Hank Liu: They have been replacing workers with machines already. Hank Liu: I saw my men being kicked out one after another. The fleet of machines kept growing. Hank Liu: It's kinda funny that I never thought I'd be the one to get the boot. Hank Liu: At least, I thought I'd be the last to get the boot. Hank Liu: Turns out the machine maintenance engineer had the last laugh.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐後躺抬手) >> Hank Liu: I was replaced by a machine that could examine the entire construction site three times per second. Hank Liu: The engineer guy said it makes better decisions than I do, by 80%. Hank Liu: The fuck does he know, huh?;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐後躺抬手返回) >> Hank Liu: A guy who's never laid a single brick before, judging my decisions? Thought: ... Hank Liu: You know what? It DOESN'T MATTER!;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐生氣) >> Hank Liu: My boss trusted him, and that's enough! Hank Liu: They said the fancy machine was oh so great, doing my job way better than myself. Hank Liu: A couple of weeks later, I didn't have a job anymore. Hank Liu: That's about the size of it.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐生氣返回) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm sorry to hear that. Hank Liu: ... Hank Liu: Thank you. ->This world doesn't need you.;<> Hank Liu: Yeah.;<< PlayUntil (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐點頭) >> Hank Liu: Don't laugh too soon, you. Hank Liu: The machines could take your job, too, you know. Thought: Technically, I don't even have this job yet... <<#EndSimple>> Hank Liu: Let's keep going. <<#EndSimple>> ->Yeah, please continue. <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Hank Liu: Like I said, my background is simple. Middle class family.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐抬手) >> Hank Liu: Bunch of work experience. Good college, too. Hank Liu: A bit long in the tooth these days, but still in awesome shape. Hank Liu: My generation grew up in some tough times. Made us tough.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐抬手返回) >> Hank Liu: Not like youngsters these days. Sissies.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐抬頭) >> Hank Liu: You can tell by the medical report. Perfect shape. Hank Liu: Anyway, I think I'm a great fit for this job. Thought: He's very confident.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐抬頭返回) >> <<#Simple(10)>> ->You said you went to a good college? Hank Liu: Right. Overqualified for a construction worker, eh?;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐驚訝) >> Hank Liu: Not the best college, mind you, but pretty good. Hank Liu: I don't know if it makes a difference, but I have the smarts, see? Hank Liu: Smart brains make more flops, no? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yes. Higher intelligence certainly helps. Hank Liu: Great!;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐驚訝返回) >> <<#Simple(6)>> ->I'm not crediting you for above-average intelligence yet.;<> Hank Liu: ... Hank Liu: Don't have to say it out loud. I know what you think and that's what counts. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->In fact, intelligence has proven no effect on flop contribution. Hank Liu: Nah, can't blame me for guessing it wrong, eh?;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐驚訝返回) >> Hank Liu: You people never let us know your standards. ->Honestly, I don't know whether intelligence has anything to do with flop contribution. Hank Liu: Really? Hank Liu: You don't know about your job very much, huh.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐驚訝返回) >> Thought: Because this technically is not my job, yet... ->I don't think you're very intelligent.;<> Hank Liu: ... Hank Liu: Okay...;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐驚訝返回) >> <<#EndSimple>> ->What's with all this confidence?;<> Hank Liu: Excuse me?;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐驚訝) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You sound as if you will definitely be selected.;<> Hank Liu: ...I'm trying to sell myself to you, okay? Hank Liu: Will you believe in me if I don't believe in myself?;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐驚訝返回) >> Thought: He has a point... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Nothing. Just a random comment. Hank Liu: Okay... ->You're being counter-productive.;<> ->No need. I won't select you.;<> <<@超兇對劉漢銘要趕他走>> Hank Liu: ... Hank Liu: Mr. Dai, please...;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐請求) >> Hank Liu: This is a matter of life and death. Please have a heart... <<#Simple(6)>> ->Have a heart, how? Hank Liu: I mean... Hank Liu: For a second you sounded like about to turn me down... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Hank Liu: Please.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐請求返回) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Shut up and make yourself scarce. <<#GoTo(@你要我劉漢銘滾)>> ->Relax, I haven't said anything yet. Hank Liu: ... Hank Liu: Good to know...;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐低頭) >> Hank Liu: Thank you. Hank Liu: So...;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐低頭返回) >> <<#GoTo(@鄰居士傑你還有沒有想問低啊)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->Let me set this straight. I can't promise you anything. Hank Liu: ... Hank Liu: Is that so? I understand...;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐驚訝返回) >> <<#Simple(6)>> ->No, you don't understand. I really don't want to see you selected.;<> <<#GoTo(@超兇對劉漢銘要趕他走)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Hank Liu: OK. Hank Liu: That's about it. I've done my part. <<@鄰居士傑你還有沒有想問低啊>> Hank Liu: Any questions?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐轉筆) >> <<@問劉漢銘問題迴圈得起始>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You have this urgent need for money. For what? ($WhyHanMinNeedMoney) <> Hank Liu: My mom's sick. Been in the hospital for a while.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐摸嘴) >> Hank Liu: I've burned through my savings just to keep her there. Hank Liu: Then the doctor told me she needs a surgery, which I can't afford. No way. Hank Liu: This is why I'm moving out of that apartment. I can't afford it.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐摸嘴返回) >> Thought: That was pretty urgent, alright... //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////有沒有租噪音抵銷機 <<#If($FinalCheckNoiseEnd < 3)>> Hank Liu: ... Hank Liu: Ah, about the noise canceler. Couldn't afford it, for the same reason. <<#Simple(6)>> ->You could have told me back then. Hank Liu: Didn't want to.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐低頭) >> Hank Liu: Telling my family issues to a guy whom I barely know? Hank Liu: Will you do that if you were me? Thought: But bombarding a neighbor with noise is totally fine to you? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Understood. Hank Liu: Yeah.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐低頭返回) >> ->Instead, you choose to bombard a neighbor with noise.;<> Hank Liu: No, I haven't.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐低頭返回) >> Hank Liu: I came knocking, right? To do what? To find out if you mind the noise. Hank Liu: I care about my neighbors, okay? <<#Simple(6)>> ->Your attitude wasn't very caring.;<> Hank Liu: I know about my attitude. Hank Liu: I said sorry, didn't I? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Hank Liu: Please, don't let it affect your judgment. Hank Liu: I really need this job. <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> ->Who do you have in the family? ($WhosHanMinFamily) <> Hank Liu: Just me and mom now. Dad passed away a few years back. Occupational injury.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐抬手) >> Hank Liu: Hey, maybe it's not so bad, being fired by Vision. Hank Liu: At least I won't die like my dad did. Hank Liu: The risk of death is real, working for Vision.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐抬手返回) >> <<#Simple(6)>> ->What do you mean? Hank Liu: They don't give a shit about workers' safety. Hank Liu: No insurance. Not replacing busted safety gear unless they absolutely have to. Can't take sick leave. Hank Liu: That's what I mean. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->I'm out of questions. Let's wrap up this session. <<#GoTo(@問完劉漢銘問題迴圈嚕啦)>> <<#EndSimple>> Hank Liu: Any more questions? Come on. <<#GoTo(@問劉漢銘問題迴圈得起始)>> <<@問完劉漢銘問題迴圈嚕啦>> Hank Liu: That settles that, then.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐待機) >> Hank Liu: Please seriously consider me. I really need this opportunity.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐請求) >> Hank Liu: ... Hank Liu: Goodbye.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_側坐請求返回) >> Thought: ... <>;<<#End>>

title: 小峰面談結束後對話
body:
Jeff Wu: Here you are!
Jeff Wu: Hey!;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐看外) >>
Jeff Wu: You're all done?
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Yeah, all done.
    Jeff Wu: Great.;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_側站比讚) >>
->Wouldn't call it "all done" yet.
    Jeff Wu: Ha, at least you have sat through all four sessions.;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_側站比讚) >>
<<#EndSimple>>
 
Jeff Wu: Now comes the tricky part. Find the best candidate out of the four.
Jeff Wu: Tough choice. Poor you.;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_側站比讚返回) >>
Jeff Wu: Being a seasoned veteran, though, I say the correct answer is pretty obvious.
Thought: He doesn't seem to be joking...
<<#Simple(6)>>
->Could you give me some hint?
    Jeff Wu: Of course.
    Jeff Wu: As long as you cover me when Auntie finds out.;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_側站抓頭) >>
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->What?
        Jeff Wu: If she finds out about this hint, she'll be mad.
    ->How bad could it be?
        Jeff Wu: You haven't seen her homicidal face yet?    
    ->Sure.
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Jeff Wu: Okay, for real now, haha.;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_側站抓頭返回) >>
    Jeff Wu: I see your job depends on this.;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_側站插腰) >>
    Jeff Wu: Hmm, let's put it this way...
    Jeff Wu: You might want to think about the whole situation the other way around.
    Jeff Wu: Exclude those who are absolutely unfit. Makes things easier.
    Jeff Wu: That's all the hint I could give.
    Jeff Wu: Or else I'd be worrying about my own job.;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_側站插腰返回) >>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
 
Jeff Wu: Feel free to go. I'll pick up from here.
Jeff Wu: Good luck, man.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐看外返回) >>
<<#End>>

title: 隆生定便當
body:
Lawrence Chang: Ah, Jason!;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_看內返回) >>
Lawrence Chang: All done with consultation?
Lawrence Chang: Thank you for the hard work!;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_側站鞠躬) >>
Thought: So much unnecessary courtesy that it's frustrating...
Lawrence Chang: I'm about to order dinner. Would you like to join?;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_側站鞠躬返回) >>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Dinner?
    Lawrence Chang: Right. It's about eight o'clock. Dinner time.
    Lawrence Chang: The company pays for it. Order what you like.
    Lawrence Chang: They say no such thing as a free lunch, but a free dinner is something, haha!
->Sure.
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: ...Let's see...;<<Set($DLState,隆生靠近點餐機)>>
Lawrence Chang: On today's menu, we have...;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看內) >>
Lawrence Chang: Huh!?
Lawrence Chang: Not Ikeshita Bento again!?;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_背站抱胸) >>
Lawrence Chang: Damn...;<<Set($DLState,廖哥看隆生定便當走過來)>>
George Liao: What's wrong with Ikeshita Bento?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看內返回) >>
Lawrence Chang: Ah, Mr. Liao.;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_背站抱胸左看) >>
Lawrence Chang: Nothing wrong, but!
Lawrence Chang: It's been the same menu for a whole week.
Lawrence Chang: Sure we deserve a treat every once in a while?
George Liao: How about MadConald's for a change?;<< Play (廖吉興, MBU_廖吉興_側站抱胸) >>
Lawrence Chang: No real difference...
George Liao: Well, don't order if it's not to your taste.
George Liao: Your dinner. Your choice.;<< Play (廖吉興, MBU_廖吉興_側站抱胸返回) >>
Lawrence Chang: OK! I get it.;<<Set($DLState,廖哥看隆生定便當走掉)>>;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_背站抱胸左看返回) >>
Lawrence Chang: *Sigh*
Lawrence Chang: I'll order for you, Jason. Which do you prefer? Chicken or pork rib?;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_背站抱胸右看) >>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Pork rib.
    Lawrence Chang: Good call, dude.
    Lawrence Chang: Their rib bento is the best.
    Lawrence Chang: Although I do get fed up after a whole week of the same thing...
    Lawrence Chang: Guess I'll order chicken today.
->Chicken.
    Lawrence Chang: But their chicken bento is kinda meh.
    Lawrence Chang: The rib is waaay better...
    Lawrence Chang: I'll let you find out yourself.
    Lawrence Chang: …Guess I'll order chicken today, too.
    Lawrence Chang: The rib bento is good, but enough is enough after a whole week.
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: Anyway, I'll bring your bento over when it arrives!
Lawrence Chang: Don't let me keep you any longer!
<<#Simple(6)>>
->Thank you.
    Lawrence Chang: Hey, my pleasure!
    Lawrence Chang: No need for this formality, man!
    Thought: Ah, this vigor of youth...;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_背站抱胸右看返回) >>
->...;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_背站抱胸右看返回) >>
<<#EndSimple>>
<<#End>>

//////////////////////////////張隆生: 好搞定，晚點我再拿便當給你！

title: 跟阿姨說自己要選誰
body: //在討論面談人物時阿姨會把他們的樣子投影出來讓人能搞清楚誰是誰。 //計算幾個人處理不好(戴勇誠政治吵到離開+傷害董欣麗+把劉漢銘氣走) //戴勇誠政治吵到離開 <<#If($EndingOfDaiYonChen = 2)>> <> <<#EndIf>> //傷害董欣麗 <<#If($EndingOfDongXinLi = 4)>> <> <<#EndIf>> //把劉漢銘氣走 <<#If($EndingOfLiuHanMin = 1)>> <> <<#EndIf>> Eva Yeh: How was it? Consultation went well?;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Pretty good, I think. ->Not very well. ->Okay-ish. <<#EndSimple>> //兩個以上態度不好 <<#If($InterviewHandleBadCount>1)>> Eva Yeh: Say no more. Eva Yeh: I'll be straightforward. So far, I'm almost sure that you are not compatible with Screening. Thought: Huh? How did she...? Eva Yeh: Your performance in consultation sessions was terrible. <<#Simple(8)>> ->How did you know? You were not there. Eva Yeh: The entire process was conducted under camera surveillance. I was watching.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內) >> Eva Yeh: I heard every word. Eva Yeh: As I said, I make sure every purchase I make is worthy. Eva Yeh: According to what I have seen, you are not worthy of your salary, even for field practice.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Eva Yeh: You are ruthless, aggressive, absolutely not fit for this position.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手) >> Eva Yeh: Frankly, I have no idea what's going on in your head. Eva Yeh: The performance is so poor that I don't see any point going over what you have done wrong. Eva Yeh: Still, I believe in going the full length. You will follow through the signing process next.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手返回) >> Eva Yeh: But don't hope to get anything out of it. You will not get this job. Eva Yeh: And I will spare you the decision-making part. Out of the four candidates, I choose Hank Liu. Thought: That jerk of a neighbor...!? Eva Yeh: Next step, you sign the contract with him. Then you officially have nothing to do with Screening. Eva Yeh: Find Wayne. He will guide you through the rest. Eva Yeh: Now, show yourself out. Thought: I... <<#Simple(8)>> ->I can do better. ->Please give me another chance. Eva Yeh: You heard me. Eva Yeh: Leave. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Thought: I screwed it up... Thought: Now Ops is my only chance. Everything depends on tomorrow... //解除主角移動，讓主角走去阿姨辦公室門口 <> <>;<<#End>> <<#EndIf>> Eva Yeh: Is that so? Eva Yeh: ...;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內) >> Thought: What's that supposed to mean...? Thought: And why does she keep checking the screen? Eva Yeh: You know... Eva Yeh: The entire process was conducted under camera surveillance. I was watching.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> Thought: WHAT!? Eva Yeh: I heard every word. Eva Yeh: As I said, I make sure every purchase I make is worthy. Thought: ... //這裡開始計算面談過程有沒有好到值得加分 <> //一個態度不好 <<#If($InterviewHandleBadCount = 1)>> //////如果是對董欣麗////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// <<#If($EndingOfDongXinLi = 4)>> Eva Yeh: First, I have a question...;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐轉筆) >> Eva Yeh: Why did you hurt Mrs. Tung? Thought: Sydney Tung, the lady who forged her medical report. Thought: I was accusing her indeed... <<#Simple(10)>> ->Not hurting. I only wanted to point out her wrongdoing. Eva Yeh: Could be. ->I lost control in the heat of the moment. Eva Yeh: You're saying that you have issue with temper management. Eva Yeh: You should have known, Mr. Dai, that temper management is a critical quality for the job you're seeking. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I screwed up. I admit. Eva Yeh: ... Eva Yeh: At least you can face your fault. ->I lost control, right. What else do you expect? Eva Yeh: Nothing. Eva Yeh: I'm only disappointed, in general. Eva Yeh: Now, listen. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Eva Yeh: Personally, I don't think what you said was wrong, not entirely.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐待機) >> Eva Yeh: But it was not the best time to say it. <<#EndIf>> //////如果是對戴勇誠////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// <<#If($EndingOfDaiYonChen = 2)>> Eva Yeh: You know what I mean. Eva Yeh: I also know that you had a fight with Eugene Dai, the first candidate. Eva Yeh: The session ended poorly over some political argument. Eva Yeh: No, "poorly" is not the right word... Eva Yeh: Unprofessionally.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐轉筆) >> <<#Simple(10)>> ->He started it. Eva Yeh: You encouraged him. Eva Yeh: Don't blame your incompetence on the occasion. Eva Yeh: Even if he is a terrible man... Eva Yeh: Your job as a consultant is to stay calm and navigate the conversation. ->It was just an exchange of opinions. Eva Yeh: Every single fight starts from an exchange of opinions. Eva Yeh: I need someone who can keep the exchange from becoming a full-on fight. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Eva Yeh: He left, because of your lack of professionalism. Eva Yeh: And you failed your job, by being not able to complete a session. Thought: ...;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐待機) >> <<#EndIf>> //////如果是對劉漢銘////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// <<#If($EndingOfLiuHanMin = 1)>> Eva Yeh: Of course, I also saw the episode between you and Hank Liu, the last candidate. Eva Yeh: Epic show. We can discuss it further if you want to. Thought: Dammit... <<#EndIf>> <<#EndIf>> //四個人都友善 <<#If($InterviewHandleBadCount = 0)>> Eva Yeh: Actually... Eva Yeh: You did well.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐前傾) >> Eva Yeh: Being reasonably courteous, at least not having a conflict with anyone. Eva Yeh: Considering this is your first time, you have passed the consultation test. ////對董欣麗特別友善 <<#If($EndingOfDongXinLi = 5)>> Eva Yeh: You felt for Mrs. Tung, who is facing real emotional hardship. <<#EndIf>> ////對戴勇誠特別友善 <<#If($EndingOfDaiYonChen = 3)>> Eva Yeh: And you made Eugene Dai trust you enough to share his personal frustrations. Eva Yeh: This is no easy feat. <<#EndIf>> Eva Yeh: Generally, I appreciate what you have done.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐前傾返回) >> Thought: Good. Now I only need to name the right candidate. <> <<#EndIf>> Eva Yeh: OK. Eva Yeh: Let's go over some details in your sessions. Eva Yeh: First of all. //戴勇誠開始 //趕走了戴勇誠 <<#If($EndingOfDaiYonChen = 1)>> Eva Yeh: You shouldn't have shown Eugene the door.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手) >> Eva Yeh: He had not made up his mind. We can't reject him like that. Eva Yeh: Be aware that the company has spent time and money on pre-screening every single candidate.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手返回) >> Eva Yeh: What you did is a waste of company resources. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I prefer to save the time for someone who's more worthy. Eva Yeh: Could be. Eva Yeh: Let's see how you treated your "more worthy" candidates, then. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@蔣瑜涵開始)>> <<#EndIf>> //有沒有跟戴勇誠討論政治話題，有談到但提早退出 。 <<#If($OverChatAboutPolitics = 1)>> Eva Yeh: The first, Eugene Dai, started a conversation about politics.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內) >> Eva Yeh: You quickly agreed with him, and brought the session back to the subject. Eva Yeh: Very well done.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> Eva Yeh: I suppose you do not really find him agreeable. Still, you held your own opinions back. <<#Simple(6)>> ->No, I actually agreed with him, for real. Eva Yeh: Oh? Eva Yeh: ... Eva Yeh: OK. I supposed wrong. <<#GoTo(@跳過你忍得住不吵政治話題很不錯)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Eva Yeh: You demonstrated a quality that I really appreciate. Eva Yeh: You have no idea how many consultants have engaged their candidates in political arguments. Eva Yeh: Have you met Jeff? Things almost got physical during his first session. <<@跳過你忍得住不吵政治話題很不錯>> <<#EndIf>> //有沒有跟戴勇誠討論政治話題，有談到但談太深後拉回來。 <<#If($OverChatAboutPolitics = 2)>> Eva Yeh: The first, Eugene Dai, started a conversation about politics. Eva Yeh: You took the bait right away. Eva Yeh: There are plenty of opportunities to distract him and keep the session going. Eva Yeh: Yet you took none of them, and engaged him in a fight instead. Eva Yeh: Pointless. Waste of time.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐後躺) >> <<#Simple(10)>> ->I tried to point out his ignorance. Eva Yeh: Did you have to? Now? Eva Yeh: Here? Eva Yeh: Specifically this man? Eva Yeh: Mr. Dai, you are doing a job. Eva Yeh: The job is about background investigation, not political education. ->I believe politics is never pointless. Eva Yeh: Technically correct, but I'm not paying you for your political views. Eva Yeh: You engaged the wrong person in a wrong fight at a wrong place. Eva Yeh: It was a waste of everyone's time. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Eva Yeh: Being a consultant, you will see many people, from all walks of life.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐後躺返回) >> Eva Yeh: Do you have to make a statement, over every single disagreement? Eva Yeh: In that case, how do you intend to do your job? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I see how I have failed. Eva Yeh: Good. ->I only wanted an exchange of opinions. Eva Yeh: Don't you get it still? Eva Yeh: If you have this urge for such "exchange", keep it outside the hours that I'm paying you for. Eva Yeh: Are we clear? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Crystal clear. ->Okay. Eva Yeh: Great. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //有走到好結局 <<#If($EndingOfDaiYonChen = 3)>> ////且前面有過政治講太深拉回來。 <<#If($OverChatAboutPolitics = 1)>> Eva Yeh: Yet again...;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內) >> Eva Yeh: You did have a little political disagreement back there. Eva Yeh: But you quickly pulled back and brought the session under control again. Eva Yeh: And...;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> <<#GoTo(@你讓戴勇誠吐露了心聲。)>> <<#EndIf>> ////且前面沒有談到政治 <<#If($OverChatAboutPolitics = 2)>> Eva Yeh: More importantly... <<@你讓戴勇誠吐露了心聲。>> Eva Yeh: You made Eugene spill his guts.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐前傾) >> Eva Yeh: He's willing to do it, because of the empathy you demonstrated. Eva Yeh: This is exactly what I look for in consultants, Mr. Dai. Eva Yeh: Show respect and empathy. Make candidates willing to speak honestly. Eva Yeh: That way, we can find the best candidate with the least trial and error. Eva Yeh: What you did has proven your capability.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐前傾返回) >> Thought: But this is not my own self. Thought: The regular me wouldn't be so nice to him. <> <<#EndIf>> <<#EndIf>> //戴勇誠平淡路線 <<#If($EndingOfDaiYonChen = 4)>> Eva Yeh: The way how you handled Eugene is remarkably good.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內) >> Eva Yeh: Especially when you asked him to stay when he tried to leave. Eva Yeh: Many consultants would have rejected on the spot, for not being able to guarantee availability. Eva Yeh: Still, you completed the entire procedure with him. Good.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> <<#EndIf>> //蔣瑜涵開始 <<@蔣瑜涵開始>> Eva Yeh: Next, Yvonne Chiang. //證件話題開始 //說不用證件 //當初要不要證件的選項給 1.要 2.不要 3.不知道，這裡「我問過」的選項要玩家問過才真的知道。 <<#If($TellYuHanNeedIDOrNot = 2)>> Eva Yeh: You told her we don't require ID.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐後躺) >> Eva Yeh: How did you know? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I asked Tyler about how consultation usually goes. ($ChouTaiMinSaidNoNeedID) Eva Yeh: The ability to proactively look for pitfalls and make preparation. Good. Eva Yeh: This is one of the purposes why we have field practice, and you have passed the test. Eva Yeh: You did truly well.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐後躺返回) >> ->It's a lucky guess. Eva Yeh: ... Eva Yeh: You're one who believes in luck. Eva Yeh: Now I have learned more about you.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐後躺返回) >> Eva Yeh: You would rather trust luck than admit your lack of knowledge. Thought: Why did I say that? This is like shooting myself in the foot... ->I just know. //如果玩家沒問曹泰銘。 <<#If($ChouTaiMinSaidNoNeedID = -1)>> Eva Yeh: In other words, a lucky guess.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐後躺返回) >> Thought: Spot-on... <<#GoTo(@去我就是知道這個選項的結束位置)>> <<#EndIf>> //如果玩家有問曹泰銘。 <<#If($ChouTaiMinSaidNoNeedID = 0)>> Eva Yeh: You asked someone, right? Thought: How does she know...? Eva Yeh: Why not be honest about it?;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐後躺返回) >> Eva Yeh: It's not a bad thing. Being able to proactively prepare for upcoming tasks is a great quality. <<@去我就是知道這個選項的結束位置>> <<#EndIf>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //說要證件 <<#If($TellYuHanNeedIDOrNot = 1)>> Eva Yeh: You told her ID is required. Eva Yeh: Unfortunately, that was incorrect.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐後躺) >> Eva Yeh: As a result, I received a complaint from Administrative, saying yet another a candidate went to them for ID verification. Eva Yeh: Worse still, she refused to go when told ID not being necessary, insisting that one of us sent her over. Eva Yeh: Given that she's desperate for this opportunity... Eva Yeh: It took a lot of time and effort to convince her otherwise.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐後躺返回) >> Eva Yeh: And that had a negative impact on the efficiency of Administrative. <<#Simple(8)>> ->But no one bothered to tell me whether ID is required or not. Eva Yeh: You still haven't caught my point. Eva Yeh: With this field practice, I'm looking for someone who is able to proactively find pitfalls and devise solutions. Eva Yeh: I do not have the resources, nor the capacity, to walk you through this step-by-step.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐手指) >> Eva Yeh: You should be able to find out what this job involves on your own, and demonstrate such ability during your field practice. Eva Yeh: So far as I can tell, you are simply not able.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐手指返回) >> Thought: This is unfair. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Expectations like that are unfair. Eva Yeh: I will pretend you didn't say that. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Eva Yeh: My point is, next time if you do not know something, say it. Eva Yeh: No, that is not good enough. Eva Yeh: Try to know everything you need to know, without being prodded. <<#EndIf>> //說不知道要不要證件 <<#If($TellYuHanNeedIDOrNot = 3)>> Eva Yeh: During the session, she asked whether or not we need to see her ID. Eva Yeh: You said you didn't know.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐後躺) >> Eva Yeh: Remember? Thought: Oops... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I do. Eva Yeh: Honesty. Good. Thought: What? Eva Yeh: When facing something you do not know, you chose to admit your lack of knowledge, instead of making random guesses. Eva Yeh: Good judgment call.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐後躺返回) >> Thought: Phew... Eva Yeh: Of course, you could have done even better. For example, trying to figure out the ID requirement, or the lack thereof, before walking into sessions. ->I...don't remember such thing? Eva Yeh: I was expecting an honest answer. Eva Yeh: When facing something you do not know, you should have chosen to admit your lack of knowledge, instead of making random guesses. Eva Yeh: Apparently I was wrong, as you are still trying to cover up for your inability.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐後躺返回) >> Thought: Shouldn't have tried to lie... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //開始討論有沒有承諾 Eva Yeh: Next. Eva Yeh: The ID issue aside. //有承諾 <<#If($PromiseYuHanOrNot = true)>> Eva Yeh: You shouldn't have made any promise to a candidate, be it positive or not.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手) >> Eva Yeh: Promising that she would be selected is bad. That she would never be selected? Not any better. Eva Yeh: Simply avoid making any promise. Eva Yeh: The reason, I think, should be self-evident enough.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手返回) >> Eva Yeh: Never make a promise that you can't keep. <<#Simple(8)>> ->But the decision is up to me. I can keep the promise. Eva Yeh: That makes your situation even worse.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐轉筆) >> Eva Yeh: You made such a promise to your second candidate. Eva Yeh: You had no way of telling whether she is the best candidate of the whole day. Eva Yeh: Or, would you argue that you already rejected the rest of the candidates before hearing them out?;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐待機) >> Thought: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //沒承諾 <<#If($PromiseYuHanOrNot = false)>> Eva Yeh: You did not make her any promise about the result. Eva Yeh: Well done.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐點頭) >> Eva Yeh: Never make a promise that you can't keep. Eva Yeh: Many consultants make things up on the fly, when the candidates repeatedly ask. Eva Yeh: At least you are not one of them. <<#EndIf>> //說年輕比較好，這裡還要判斷有沒有問過隆生 <<#If($TellYuHanYoungGood = true)>> Eva Yeh: By the way, younger candidates are not necessarily better. Keep that in mind. Eva Yeh: The purpose of consultation is to make comprehensive assessments. <<#If($HelmetSaidYoungGood = 0)>> Eva Yeh: Wait. Who gave you such idea, to begin with? Eva Yeh: You must have heard it from somebody. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Lawrence told me. ($HelmetSaidYoungGood) <> <<#GoTo(@張龍生那小子亂說什麼年輕)>> ->No. I deduced out of common sense. Eva Yeh: Is that so? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah. Eva Yeh: Okay. ->OK, no. Lawrence told me. <<@張龍生那小子亂說什麼年輕>> Eva Yeh: That boy... Eva Yeh: I thought he should have learned better.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內) >> Eva Yeh: ...;//阿姨按了通訊按鈕 Eva Yeh: Edith. Tell Lawrence to come here afterwards. Eva Yeh: ... Eva Yeh: No, not a friendly talk. Eva Yeh: He has some education due. Thought: Sorry, Lawrence...;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >>;//之後張龍生超級道歉，玩家以為會被罵但是反而很內疚蔣了錯誤的訊息 <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> <<#EndIf>> //董欣麗開始 Eva Yeh: Next.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內) >>;//看螢幕 Eva Yeh: Sydney Tung. //傷害她。 <<#If($EndingOfDongXinLi = 4)>> Eva Yeh: Like I said when we began this conversation, you hurt her.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> Thought: Yeah, I did... Eva Yeh: By the regulations, you should have rejected on the spot.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手) >> Eva Yeh: At her age, flop contribution will almost definitely end up in a disaster. Eva Yeh: On top of that, she forged personal records. Thought: My bad...;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手返回) >> ////有問話 <<#If($AskDongXinLiHowSheIn = true)>> Eva Yeh: But. You took the opportunity, trying to find out who slipped her through. Eva Yeh: Indeed, we must find out who her insider friend is. Eva Yeh: Maybe you didn't find anything useful. Eva Yeh: But the initiative itself counts. You did the right thing, above and beyond your responsibility. <<#EndIf>> <<#EndIf>> //一開始就請走她 <<#If($EndingOfDongXinLi = 1)>> Eva Yeh: You rejected Mrs. Tung. Good judgment call.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> Eva Yeh: She is obviously not qualified, and she violated regulations.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手) >> Eva Yeh: You could have done better, though, by asking her to stay. Eva Yeh: Just keep the conversation going. You might find something helpful. Who knows? Eva Yeh: Don't worry about it, though. I will send someone else to investigate her insider friend.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手返回) >> <<#EndIf>> //有留下來面談(且好結局) <<#If($EndingOfDongXinLi = 5)>> <<#GoTo(@好結局，然後應該請董欣麗離開)>> <<#EndIf>> //有留下來面談 <<#If($EndingOfDongXinLi = 2)>> <<@好結局，然後應該請董欣麗離開>> Eva Yeh: You should have rejected her on the spot. Eva Yeh: Instead, you gave her false hope, knowing she had no chance at all.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> Eva Yeh: Hearing her personal story will only hinder your decision process. //////(沒調查) <<#If($AskDongXinLiHowSheIn = false)>> Eva Yeh: Now that she decided to stay, you could have tried to find out how she slipped through the screening process. <<#GoTo(@董欣麗面談檢討結束)>> <<#EndIf>> //////(有調查) <<#If($AskDongXinLiHowSheIn = true)>> Eva Yeh: You did take the opportunity to ask questions. Good.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐前傾) >> Eva Yeh: We must find out how she managed to cheat. Eva Yeh: Maybe you didn't find anything useful. Eva Yeh: But the initiative itself counts. You did the right thing, above and beyond your responsibility.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐前傾返回) >> Eva Yeh: Well done, Jason.;<< PlayUntil (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐點頭) >> <<#EndIf>> //好結局路線 <<#If($EndingOfDongXinLi = 5)>> Eva Yeh: ... Eva Yeh: Everything else aside. Eva Yeh: I do appreciate how you treated her.;<< PlayUntil (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐點頭) >> Eva Yeh: I myself have a similar situation, being separated from my family. I can relate...;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內) >> Eva Yeh: Having someone to talk to, like she did back there, means more than you know to her. Thought: She's softening a little... Eva Yeh: ... Eva Yeh: OK. That does not change the principle. If she's not qualified, she's not qualified.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> Thought: Nah, forget about the soften part. <> <<#EndIf>> <<#EndIf>> //有留下來調查 <<#If($EndingOfDongXinLi = 3)>> Eva Yeh: I appreciate how you handled her case.;<< PlayUntil (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐點頭) >> Eva Yeh: Keep her talking, trying to find out how she cheated the screening system.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐低頭) >> Eva Yeh: Then asking her to leave, instead of going on with the session. Eva Yeh: I couldn't expect any better. Eva Yeh: Well done, Jason.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐低頭返回) >> <<#EndIf>> <<@董欣麗面談檢討結束>> //劉漢銘開始 Eva Yeh: Last, Mr. Hank Liu. //在主角家吵過架 <<#If($FinalCheckBotherEnd >-1)>> //吵過架且因為這件事趕人家走 <<#If($EndingOfLiuHanMin = 1)>> Thought: ... Eva Yeh: You kicked him out because of some personal grudge. <<#Simple(4)>> ->He offered to leave himself. Thought: He did apologize, yeah... Eva Yeh: You were blinded by personal grudge.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手返回) >> Eva Yeh: It was very unprofessional, if you want my official opinion. <<#EndIf>> //吵過架且沒有因為這件事趕人家走 <<#If($EndingOfLiuHanMin = 2 )>> Eva Yeh: You met an acquaintance. What a surprise. Eva Yeh: Small world, isn't it? ////態度不好 <<#If($HurtLiuHanMinCount > 3)>> Eva Yeh: Yet I do notice hostility on your side throughout the session. Everything is handled as it should be.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> <<#EndIf>> <<#EndIf>> Eva Yeh: OK. So much for review. Eva Yeh: I've said my part. Now it's your turn.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐轉筆) >> Eva Yeh: Tell me, who do you think is the best candidate? Thought: I have this one chance. Don't blow it! <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Eugene Dai. //跟戴勇誠政治吵架 <<#If($EndingOfDaiYonChen = 2)>> Eva Yeh: You had one heck of a fight with him, and didn't finish the session. Eva Yeh: But now you're telling me he is the candidate?;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐待機) >> Eva Yeh: Why? <<#GoTo(@幹嘛選戴勇誠啊？)>> <<#EndIf>> //直接趕他走。 <<#If($EndingOfDaiYonChen = 1)>> Eva Yeh: This is a truly peculiar decision.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐待機) >> Eva Yeh: You are selecting someone you hardly talked to? <<@幹嘛選戴勇誠啊？>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Based on intuition. Eva Yeh: Intuition is real when it comes to judgment calls.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐後躺) >> Eva Yeh: A lot of times what we call "intuition" is in fact distilled knowledge and experience. Eva Yeh: Maybe you don't really want this job so much.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐後躺返回) >> <<#Simple(6)>> ->No. I seriously, really, want to have this job. Eva Yeh: In that case, don't make random guesses next time. If you do have a next time, that is. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Because others are obviously unfit. Eva Yeh: Sydney Tung, unfit indeed, because of her age.;<< PlayUntil (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐點頭) >> Eva Yeh: But why is Yvonne Chiang unfit?;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內) >> Eva Yeh: Especially, when the system's pre-screening gave her a fairly high rating? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->She's too young. Eva Yeh: I'm not sure where you get this idea about being "too young". Eva Yeh: Truth is, there's no such thing.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> ->Personality-wise, she is immature. Eva Yeh: That has no bearing on her performance as a contributor.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> ->She has an upcoming exam. ($AskedAboutSequelae);//////////////////////////////////////////////////有問到這項情報才會顯示 Eva Yeh: ...;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> Eva Yeh: Correct. Flop contribution will have a negative impact on her.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐低頭) >> Eva Yeh: So much that she won't be able to think straight by the time of said exam. Eva Yeh: You learned about the side effects of flop contribution on your own.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐低頭返回) >> Eva Yeh: I'm pleasantly surprised. Thought: Yay! She's really impressed! Eva Yeh: However, Eugene Dai is not the correct answer.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手) >> Thought: Ah... Eva Yeh: Because he showed reluctance at the beginning of the session. Eva Yeh: We have someone else with similar rating from pre-screening, and guaranteed availability.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手返回) >> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //沒有吵架 <<#If($EndingOfDaiYonChen > 2)>> Eva Yeh: Good choice.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐待機) >> Thought: Great. Eva Yeh: However, he is not the best.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐低頭) >> Thought: What? Eva Yeh: Yes, he meets our requirement in all metrics. ////有進好結局。 <<#If($EndingOfDaiYonChen = 3)>> Eva Yeh: You had an in-depth conversation with him, from which we learned he does need this opportunity. <<#EndIf>> Eva Yeh: However, he was reluctant, a fact that we must not overlook.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐低頭返回) >> <<#Simple(6)>> ->It doesn't mean he will forfeit. Eva Yeh: You are correct.;<< PlayUntil (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐點頭) >> Eva Yeh: However, we have someone else on the record, with similar metrics, and will surely not forfeit. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> ->Yvonne Chiang. Eva Yeh: A good decision.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐待機) >> Eva Yeh: Sadly, I won't call it the best.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐低頭) >> Eva Yeh: You do remember she has an upcoming exam, don't you? Eva Yeh: Do you know what flop contribution will do to her? Eva Yeh: Under side effects, she will not be able to think straight for a while. Eva Yeh: If we choose her, she will surely fail the most important exam of her lifetime.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐低頭返回) >> //玩家不知道後遺症設定的話進來這段(沒問到這項情報才會顯示) <<#If($AskedAboutSequelae = -1)>> Thought: Side effects? <<#Simple(6)>> ->Flop contribution has side effects? Eva Yeh: Unfortunately, yes.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐點頭) >> Eva Yeh: Short-term contributors, like this vacancy we are trying to fill, are only affected for a short while.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手) >> Eva Yeh: If the service duration stretches into, let's say, one year, the symptoms could be permanent. Eva Yeh: Such symptoms include but are not limited to dizziness and memory loss.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手返回) >> Thought: So, you know, only those truly desperate will volunteer for flop contribution. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Eva Yeh: It's a shame to say so, but I believe... ->Sydney Tung. //沒對她友好到最後 <<#If($EndingOfDongXinLi < 5)>> Thought: Hey hey hey what was that!? Thought: No way! She can't possibly make the cut! Eva Yeh: An overaged candidate, who cheated the system?;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐待機) >> Eva Yeh: This is, most obviously, an incorrect answer. <<#EndIf>> //有對她友好到最後 <<#If($EndingOfDongXinLi = 5)>> Eva Yeh: ...;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐待機) >> Eva Yeh: You are doing this out of sympathy, I trust. Eva Yeh: But believe me, you don't want her as a contributor.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內) >> Eva Yeh: She can't survive the process. Thought: What is the process exactly? How tough is it...? Eva Yeh: On the other hand...;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> <<#EndIf>> ->Hank Liu.;<> Thought: She's smiling.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐待機) >> //吵到他走 <<#If($EndingOfLiuHanMin = 1)>> <<#GoTo(@我很訝異你會選擇他。也就是劉漢銘)>> <<#EndIf>> //吵到他有敵意 <<#If($HurtLiuHanMinCount > 3)>> <<@我很訝異你會選擇他。也就是劉漢銘>> Eva Yeh: I'm surprised that you choose him. Eva Yeh: It's the correct answer, though. <<#GoTo(@劉漢銘才是最終正確人選壓)>> <<#EndIf>> //沒有吵到他走或有敵意(不用特別if了) Eva Yeh: Yes. <<#EndSimple>> <<@劉漢銘才是最終正確人選壓>> Eva Yeh: Hank Liu is our best candidate. //吵到他走或有敵意 <<#If($EndingOfLiuHanMin = 1)>> <<#GoTo(@哎呀你居然選對嚕啦嘿)>> <<#EndIf>> <<#If($HurtLiuHanMinCount > 3)>> <<@哎呀你居然選對嚕啦嘿>> Eva Yeh: I suppose you don't like him. Eva Yeh: Not so hard to tell, based on your attitude throughout the session. <<#EndIf>> Eva Yeh: Every candidate has their own issues.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內) >> Eva Yeh: Eliminating those who are absolutely not fit, Hank Liu is the last one remaining. Eva Yeh: Good health. Mentally stable. Predictable life. Eva Yeh: And he has a legitimate need for this opportunity, to ease the financial stress from unemployment. Thought: I thought she said we should prioritize those the most in need.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> <<#Simple(8)>> ->But Hank is not the most desperate. Eva Yeh: You're correct that he is not.;<< PlayUntil (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐點頭) >> Eva Yeh: But he is desperate enough, am I wrong?;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐前傾) >> Eva Yeh: The bottom line is, we are running a business, not a charity organization. Eva Yeh: You, and I too, must answer to a boss. Eva Yeh: We find the middle ground as best as we can.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐前傾返回) >> Thought: This is different from that lecture she gave Sky Qiu... Thought: Come to think of it, she is sorta a hypocrite. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I agree with Sky Qiu. You don't really care about candidates. Thought: Do I have to say that out loud!? Eva Yeh: ... Eva Yeh: Questioning me. A commendable show of courage. Eva Yeh: ... Eva Yeh: I do care about them. Eva Yeh: Mrs. Tung is too fragile for flop contribution.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐低頭) >> Eva Yeh: Ms. Chiang can't afford the side effects, if her upcoming exam is critical. Eva Yeh: Mr. Dai, Eugene, is not sure about what he wants. He could be a waste of time. Eva Yeh: Mr. Liu is the best common ground, with everyone's best interest in mind. Eva Yeh: Now, allow me to make my statement again.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐低頭返回) >> Eva Yeh: Hopefully you will have a better understanding. Eva Yeh: I, Eva Yeh, care about every single candidate, care for them. Thought: ... Thought: She looks kind of... Thought: ...wavering. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> //選劉漢銘 <<#If($FinalChooseLiu = true)>> Eva Yeh: With everything considered, you did well for naming the best candidate. Eva Yeh: Solid performance. Thought: Great! I now have much more leverage! Eva Yeh: Of course, maybe you really prefer someone else... <<#EndIf>> Eva Yeh: Despite your personal preference, it is what it is.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內) >> Eva Yeh: I guess someone in the office must have told you making the right decision is the key to your field practice. Eva Yeh: And that this "someone", I suppose, might very well be Tyler. Thought: Yep. Eva Yeh: In fact, the entire process is a test, from consultation sessions to the final decision. Eva Yeh: Now I have everything I need to know.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內返回) >> //阿姨覺得主角友善 <<#If($InterviewHandleBadCount = 0)>> Thought: Based on her reaction, I should be doing pretty well... <<#EndIf>> //阿姨覺得普通 <<#If($InterviewHandleBadCount = 1)>> Thought: I can't quite tell what she's thinking, though... <<#EndIf>> Eva Yeh: Your next step is to sign the contract with Hank Liu.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手) >> Eva Yeh: Like last time, Wayne will walk you through the process. Eva Yeh: Unlike last time, however, he will sit through the signing process.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐抬手返回) >> Eva Yeh: We can't afford to fully entrust this final step to new employees. Thought: I don't mind. Having a friendly senior by my side is a big plus. Eva Yeh: Oh. Eva Yeh: On top of Hank, there's someone else I need you to sign contract with. Eva Yeh: She has been pre-screened and verified, by myself. Eva Yeh: You must sign her on. Understand?;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐手指) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yes, ma'am. Eva Yeh: Great. ->What kind of person are we talking about? Eva Yeh: That is not important. Eva Yeh: Bear this in mind, Mr. Dai. Eva Yeh: Sign her on, or you will be in big trouble. <<#EndSimple>> Eva Yeh: Come back when all is done. I will give the final verdict.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐手指返回) >> Thought: This is my last chance for Screening. Don't screw it up! Eva Yeh: Go, meet Wayne in the office. Eva Yeh: See you later, Mr. Dai. Eva Yeh: ... Eva Yeh: Edith, show Lawrence in.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐看內) >> //if計算阿姨信任度，前面有兩段善良好品德就加1分 <<#If($DLStateFloat>1)>> <> <<#EndIf>> <<#End>>

title: 問隆生問題
body:
<<#Simple(10)>>
->Hi.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_背站轉頭右) >>
    <<#GoTo(@開始第一次問安全帽問題)>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
<<#End>>
 
<<@開始第一次問安全帽問題>>
Lawrence Chang: Hi. Can I help you?;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_側坐轉頭)>>
<<@第一次循環問安全帽問題>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Just curious, what do you do here? ($AskHelmetWhatDoesHeDo)
    <<Set($AskHelmetWhatDoesHeDo, -1)>>
    Lawrence Chang: Ah, consultant, like Tyler.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_側坐轉頭手指)>>
    Lawrence Chang: Feel free to call me senpai!
    Lawrence Chang: Kidding. Haha! I'm about as new as you are.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_側坐轉頭手指返回)>>
->Any advice on screening? ($AskHelmetHowToPick)
    <<Set($AskHelmetHowToPick, -1)>>;<<Set($HelmetSaidYoungGood,0)>>
    Lawrence Chang: Advice? Lemme think...;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_側坐轉頭轉回)>>
    Lawrence Chang: Ah!
    Lawrence Chang: I'd say go for the younger ones.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_側坐轉頭轉回返回)>>
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->Are you sure?
        Lawrence Chang: Mostly sure. I go by this principle and haven't failed yet!
        Lawrence Chang: Younger means healthier. Better performance as contributor.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_側坐轉頭手指)>>
        Lawrence Chang: They usually get better rating in pre-screening, too!
        Thought: He's quite confident.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_側坐轉頭手指返回)>>
    ->Thank you.
        Lawrence Chang: You're welcome!
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
->I don't have any more questions.
    Lawrence Chang: OK!;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_側坐轉頭返回) >>
    <<#End>>
<<#EndSimple>>
 
Lawrence Chang: Anything else?
<<#GoTo(@第一次循環問安全帽問題)>>

title: 問慈恩問題
body:
<<#Simple(10)>>
->Hello.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_背站轉頭左)>>
    <<#GoTo(@開始第一次問葉慈恩嗯嗯問題)>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
<<#End>>
 
 
<<@開始第一次問葉慈恩嗯嗯問題>>
Edith Yeh: Hi. Can I help you?;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭) >>
<<@第一次循環問慈嗯嗯嗯問題>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->How is the vibe here in the office? ($AskYehTsuEnWorkPlace)
    <<Set($AskYehTsuEnWorkPlace, -1)>>
    Edith Yeh: It's, um, up to personal preference?
    Edith Yeh: I quite enjoy working here, myself.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭抬手)>>
    Edith Yeh: Everyone gets along.
    Edith Yeh: The manager can be demanding sometimes, but that's just the way she is.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭抬手返回)>>
    Edith Yeh: We all trust her.
    Edith Yeh: I'm not trying to be diplomatic, you know!
    Edith Yeh: What do you think, so far?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->I like it.
        Edith Yeh: Great!
    ->I'm not quite used to it yet.
        Edith Yeh: Understandable.
        Edith Yeh: After all, you haven't been here even for a day yet.
        Edith Yeh: It gets better over time.
    ->I'm not sure.
        Edith Yeh: Give it some more time. No rush.
        Edith Yeh: You have years ahead.
        Edith Yeh: Ah, presuming you pass the field practice, that is.
        Thought: ...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Edith Yeh: Oh right, the job benefits have improved since the new supervisor arrived.
    Edith Yeh: I haven't met her yet, the new supervisor from HQ, but she has already done quite a few favors for us Downstairs divisions.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭轉回)>>
    Edith Yeh: Previous supervisors only cared about Upstairs, pretending we don't exist.
    Edith Yeh: Now we finally have an advocate.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭轉回返回)>>
->Can you give me any advice on screening candidates? ($AskYehTsuEnHowToPick)
    <<Set($AskYehTsuEnHowToPick, -1)>>
    <<Set($AskedAboutSequelae, 0)>>
    Edith Yeh: Hmmm...
    Edith Yeh: No.
    Thought: What? Why?
    <<#Simple(6)>>
    ->May I know why?
        Edith Yeh: Don't jump to conclusions yet.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Edith Yeh: I said no because Ms. Yeh won't allow it.
    Edith Yeh: She wants to see how you operate of your own accord.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭抬手)>>
    Edith Yeh: Giving advice would affect your own judgment.
    Thought: How am I supposed to...;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭抬手返回)>>
    Edith Yeh: Although...
    Edith Yeh: There's one thing I can tell you, I suppose.
    Edith Yeh: All candidates you will meet today are applying for short-term contributor opportunities.
    Edith Yeh: By "short-term", I mean one week or two.
    Edith Yeh: Nobody would volunteer, if not for an urgent need of cash.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭轉回)>>
    Edith Yeh: That is because short-term contribution tends to have powerful side effects.
    Edith Yeh: After service, they will experience dizziness, confusion, or memory loss for over a month.
    Edith Yeh: Those who have big plans upcoming may not be the best fit.
    Edith Yeh: Um... Am I giving too much information?;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭轉回返回)>>
    Edith Yeh: Ms. Yeh won't be happy if she finds out.
    <<#Simple(6)>>
    ->I won't tell.
        Edith Yeh: Deal!
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
->I don't have any more questions.
    Edith Yeh: Good.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭返回) >>
    <<#End>>
<<#EndSimple>>
Edith Yeh: OK. Anything else you want to know?
<<#GoTo(@第一次循環問慈嗯嗯嗯問題)>>

title: 面談完回到阿姨辦公室
body:
Edith Yeh: So, HQ doesn't want to give the green light...
//阿姨抬頭注意到主角進來了
Eva Yeh: You're back.;<< Play (葉宜樺, MBU_葉宜樺_側坐後躺) >>
Edith Yeh: Oh, hi, Jason.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_看外) >>
Thought: The atmosphere is depressing.
Eva Yeh: What's the problem with Silencio? Why does it keep letting uninvited people in?

body:
Lawrence Chang: Oh hi!

<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Not bad.
    Lawrence Chang: Great!
->Poorly.

<<#EndSimple>>

<<#If($HelmetSaidYoungGood = -10)>>

    Thought: Probably because how he misled me.;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_側站抬頭返回) >>

    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->Probably because of me. I'm sorry.
        Lawrence Chang: Eh? What for?;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_側站慌張) >>
        <<#Simple(99999)>>
        ->For telling Ms. Yeh that you gave me incorrect information.
        ->Actually, prioritizing young candidates is wrong.
            Lawrence Chang: Huh...!?
            Lawrence Chang: What!? For real!?
            <<@隆生因為年紀事件猛爆性道歉>>
            Lawrence Chang: Sorry! For feeding you the wrong idea!;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_側站道歉) >>
            Lawrence Chang: Did you flunk your sessions because of me?
            Lawrence Chang: Aaaaahhhh!
            Lawrence Chang: I'm so very, very sorry!

            Lawrence Chang: ...Ah, I see!;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_側站道歉返回) >>
            Lawrence Chang: I'm gonna tell Auntie it's all my fault. You were just misled!
            Lawrence Chang: I'm going to her office right away!;<< PlayUntil (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_側站點頭) >>
            <<Set($HelmetSaidYoungGood,-20)>>;<<#End>>
        <<#EndSimple>>
    ->The thing you told me was wrong.

        Lawrence Chang: Huh!?;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_側站慌張) >>

<<#EndIf>>

title: 被鄭緯叫醒
body:
Wayne Zheng: Jason?;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_簽約正坐左看) >>
Wayne Zheng: Here you are!
Wayne Zheng: Sorry, didn't know you were taking a nap.
Wayne Zheng: Hopefully I didn't ruin it.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約正坐右看) >>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->No biggie. I'm awake now.
    Wayne Zheng: Great.;<< PlayUntil (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_簽約正坐左看點頭) >>
->Can I go back to sleep now?
    Wayne Zheng: Haha, no way!
    Wayne Zheng: Rise and shine, sleepyhead. We have contracts to sign.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Wayne Zheng: You're not used to night shifts, are you? Otherwise you shouldn't feel drowsy this early.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_簽約正坐左看返回) >>
Thought: That I'm not used to, yeah.
//張隆生走過來
<<Set($DLState,張隆生走過來)>>
Lawrence Chang: Yo! Wayne, Jason! How's it going?
Wayne Zheng: Lawrence? What are you doing here?;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_簽約正坐左看) >>
Lawrence Chang: To cover Tyler's shift.
Lawrence Chang: He got the time wrong. Went out but couldn't make it back before signing starts.
Lawrence Chang: I'll wait here with you guys for a while.
<<#End>>

title: 調查物件:簽約走廊細則
簽約室門前問鄭緯問題
body:
Wayne Zheng: OK. Get ready. We're going in.
Wayne Zheng: If you have any questions, this is your last chance to ask.
<<@第一次簽約室外問政緯問題>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Can they refuse to sign the contract? ($AskWillTheyRejectToSign)
    <<Set($AskWillTheyRejectToSign,-1)>>
    Wayne Zheng: Of course, it's their decision. Normally they wouldn't have a reason to say no, though.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站點頭) >>
    Wayne Zheng: To streamline the process, the company wants us to navigate the situation in the rare event that they change their mind.
    Wayne Zheng: By the way, this will affect your performance review.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站比一) >>
    Wayne Zheng: Failing to sign a candidate on, usually means an in-depth talk with your manager.
    Thought: Don't let them change their mind. Noted.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站比一返回) >>
->Can I modify terms of the contract? ($AskMayIChangeTheCondition)
    <<Set($AskMayIChangeTheCondition,-1)>>
    Wayne Zheng: If they insist?
    Wayne Zheng: No. The contract terms are firm. Not even the manager could make any change.
    Wayne Zheng: Our job is to convince the candidates to accept the deal.
    Thought: No room for negotiation, then...
->I don't have any more questions.
    Wayne Zheng: OK. Let's begin.;<< PlayUntil (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站點頭) >>
    <<#End>>
<<#EndSimple>>
 
Wayne Zheng: Any more questions?
<<#GoTo(@第一次簽約室外問政緯問題)>>

<<#End>>

Flop contribution is my last resort.;<< Play (劉漢銘, MBU_劉漢銘_簽約側坐後傾) >> Hank Liu: Ah... Hank Liu: Don't get me wrong. Got nothing against contribution, just not used to the idea of it.
