title: 跟程芳剛上頂樓
body:
Frances Cheng: Here we are!
Frances Cheng: Ah, I love this night breeze!
Frances Cheng: Here! Over here!
<<#End>>
//芳興奮地跑到觀景地點。

title: 程芳在頂樓觀景位置
body: //public float HowDoYouDefineGOrB = 1; //public float GOrBWhatAreYouTalking = 1; //public bool PromiseFangGoParade = falsr; Frances Cheng: How is it? Thought: How is what?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->How is what? Frances Cheng: The view, dummy. Thought: Brighter than expected. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Brighter than expected. Frances Cheng: What kind of comment is that? Haha! ->It's breathtaking. Frances Cheng: Right? <<#EndSimple>> ->It's breathtaking. Frances Cheng: Right? <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: I come here every once in a while.;<> Frances Cheng: From here I can see the HQ tower.;<> Frances Cheng: The sight of it reminds me of things... Thought: Fran used to work there, where she never felt happy. Frances Cheng: Back then I thought I could see all the way to the end of my life, work and more work.;<> Frances Cheng: Until you showed up.;<> Thought: ...;<> Frances Cheng: I've been thinking lately... Frances Cheng: ...that you have changed me, a lot. Frances Cheng: Once I was so eager, trying my best to prove...;<> Frances Cheng: My worth. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Prove, to whom? Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Funny, I never asked myself that, not even once. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Everything I did was to earn myself the peace of mind. Frances Cheng: Because I was constantly afraid that...;<> Frances Cheng: Maybe someday, out of the blue, someone would show up and tell everyone that I'm a fake. Frances Cheng: That I'm nothing. I don't deserve anything I own. Frances Cheng: That I'm the child that no one ever is proud of.;<> Thought: ... Frances Cheng: This someone never appeared. Frances Cheng: Instead, I found you.;<> Frances Cheng: You made me see how lost I was. Frances Cheng: Although you don't like to give advice... Frances Cheng: You kind of made me see myself, see what I really want. Frances Cheng: Now I want to help others, by giving what they need when they need it. Frances Cheng: Like what you did for me.;<> Thought: No, Fran... Thought: Quite the opposite, you saved me...;<> Frances Cheng: This is why I asked to be transferred here. Frances Cheng: I hope to help these contributors, by finding a way that is reasonably profitable, yet much more humane. Frances Cheng: Or so I believe. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Ah, sorry! I've been going on and on about myself.;<> Frances Cheng: You are the star of the show today, J.;<> Frances Cheng: How did things go? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->The interview evolved into field practice. No verdict yet. Frances Cheng: Wow, Ms. Yeh surely leaves no stone unturned. ->Today I tried out every single thing under Screening. Frances Cheng: Must be hard, figuring out everything on the fly.;<> Frances Cheng: I'm surprised, that Ms. Yeh wanted hands-on test on literally everything, on the first day. <> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: How do you feel about her, so far? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Adamant when it comes to her principles. Frances Cheng: Yes, exactly. I feel the same.;<> Frances Cheng: You should have witnessed first-hand, that this line of work involves difficult decisions. Frances Cheng: Being "adamant", after so many years in the business, is a rare quality. Frances Cheng: I suppose you like her management style? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah, she's pretty good. Frances Cheng: Great. ->Sort of. Frances Cheng: You sound forced. Haha! ->Nope. Don't like her at all. Frances Cheng: Haha! Frances Cheng: Forget that I asked. <<#EndSimple>> ->She forces her standards on others. Frances Cheng: I know where you're coming from, based on what I've seen myself... Frances Cheng: This could take some getting used to. <<#Simple(6)>> ->She's not bad as a person. That attitude, though, not so much. Frances Cheng: In that case, you probably won't like it, if hired. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Hey, the most important thing. Frances Cheng: Did you... Frances Cheng: Did you get the job? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No idea yet, with everything interrupted by the psychopath. Frances Cheng: Ah...;<> Frances Cheng: Right. She has enough to deal with already. <> ->I'll probably find out tomorrow. Frances Cheng: Is that so? <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Wait. They want you to be here again, tomorrow? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yes, because I only tried out Screening today. ->Yeah. Tomorrow is the field practice for Ops. Frances Cheng: Wait!;<> Frances Cheng: I just realized, didn't you say this interview was for Ops? Frances Cheng: Why did they ask you to try out Screening today? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Ms. Yeh says she wants to see which is the better fit. Frances Cheng: Sounds like she doesn't trust Silencio's decision very much...;<> Frances Cheng: Makes sense, given her age... <<#Simple(6)>> ->Yeah, oldsters are like that. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: People from her generation tend not to trust AI. Frances Cheng: But it's not a bad thing! Now you have double the chances. ->Simply put, I have equal chance for both Screening and Ops. Frances Cheng: Isn't that great!;<> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Show them your best! Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: So, everything you did today was for Screening, J? Thought: Suddenly she's getting serious... Frances Cheng: I guess you have met the people we regularly sign contracts with...;<> Frances Cheng: And the man who tried to kill you... Frances Cheng: What do you make of it? Thought: What does she mean? Frances Cheng: This career that I'm in...;<> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: The flop farming business as a whole. Are we preying on the disadvantaged...?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sort of. ->I don't think so. ->This is not a yes or no question. Frances Cheng: ...You think so? ->Why ask? Frances Cheng: Because I have been questioning myself... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: So far, I have found no answer.;<> Frances Cheng: I came here with the hope to help people. Frances Cheng: The more I do, the less I believe in what I'm doing.;<> Frances Cheng: Maybe what I do doesn't matter. Nothing could change the industry. Frances Cheng: Maybe this whole industry is built on the exploitation of the poorest people. Thought: Fran...;<> Frances Cheng: Aaaaahhhh!;<> Frances Cheng: What should I do!!!! Thought: I should calm her down...;<> Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: To tell you the truth, J. I'm living in constant fear.;<> Frances Cheng: Fear that what I believe is "for the better" turns out to be just as bad. Frances Cheng: I don't want to be a snob who's full of fancy talk. Frances Cheng: I'll really hate myself for it.;<> Thought: Fran, you're overthinking it.;<> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: What if I told you I want to quit?;<> Frances Cheng: If someday I do find the answer to my question, and it means I must quit this job? Thought: Whatever you decide, I'm always by your side, Fran.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'd say go for it. Don't worry about money. Leave it to me. ->Your call, Fran. I'm always by your side. <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Thank you. //一陣風吹過，樹葉搖擺，遠方有警笛聲(有點放空的氛圍) <<#End>> //這裡的文本之後用在別的地方，吵參加遊行放在第二天早上，吵運算產業放在隔天辦公室/////////////////////////////////////////////////// Frances Cheng: Look at me, too afraid to ask for your opinion. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Afraid? Of what? Frances Cheng: Because there's a chance that... Frances Cheng: ...that what you say will make me feel lost again. Thought: ...? Frances Cheng: I'm afraid that you're finally seeing the ugly side of my industry, my job. Frances Cheng: And you may hate me for being a part of it. <<#Simple(6)>> ->What are you talking about, Fran? Frances Cheng: I'm not sure if I'm doing the right thing. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I'm a supervisor, an executive, but I know how this farm works on the microscopic level. Frances Cheng: I've been to Downstairs and seen how every link fit together. Frances Cheng: I know too well about the kind of people you deal with at Screening... Frances Cheng: Everyone, every single of them, has a sad story. Frances Cheng: They are here to sell the last shred of dignity, the only thing they have. Frances Cheng: And what we do at this farm is essentially to tell them that the only thing they have isn't worth much. Frances Cheng: This is how the farm generates profit. Frances Cheng: I have this feeling, J, that no matter how hard I try to convince myself... Frances Cheng: ...at the end of the day, I'm still helping bad people to exploit the poor. Frances Cheng: The man who tried to stab you, I guess he only wanted revenge for something we took from him... Thought: ... Frances Cheng: The reason I petitioned for a transfer from HQ is to advocate for their wellbeing. Frances Cheng: But so far, every hour, every day, I'm doubting myself... Thought: Don't blame yourself for other people's fault, Fran. <<#Simple(10)>> ->None of this is your fault. Frances Cheng: I didn't cause any of this, yes. Frances Cheng: But I want to make things better, so... Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: It will be devastating if someone tells me that I'm only making things worse. ->You have tried. Frances Cheng: That I have. Frances Cheng: It's just that... Frances Cheng: Am I trying in the right direction? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: You saw it with your own eyes, J. Frances Cheng: Tell me what you think, please. Frances Cheng: About contributors, flop farming, Vision. About me... Frances Cheng: Am I really helping, by maintaining the status quo, by contributing to it? Thought: Honestly, I have no idea. This is way beyond me... <<#Simple(6)>> ->I'm not involved enough to have an opinion. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: You're right... Frances Cheng: You are never troubled by lofty ideas, J. Frances Cheng: Sometimes you make me jealous. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Not fair. <<#Simple(6)>> ->What? Frances Cheng: Nothing. It's just that... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: You changed me so much, but I hardly left a dent on you. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Is that a bad thing? Frances Cheng: Nope. Only feeling a bit unfair, haha. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Anyhow, I love you for who you are, right now. That is good enough! Frances Cheng: My bad, taking the conversation to weird directions. Sorry. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Sorry, I don't have an answer to give. Frances Cheng: Don't worry. I wasn't expecting any. Frances Cheng: This is a question that I must find answers, on my own. Frances Cheng: Asking you would be cheating. ->Why are we talking about this? Frances Cheng: Because I'm about to make a big decision. Frances Cheng: Hey, don't overthink it! Frances Cheng: It's about work, not us! Haha. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@程芳頂樓主題嚴肅對話結束)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Am I doing the right thing, by making myself a part of this industry? <<@程芳問她參與算力供給產業是不是對的？>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No. Sometimes we have to live with the necessary evil. Frances Cheng: The necessary evil? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Think. If flop farming doesn't exist, they wouldn't even have this line of income. <<@是萬象害這些人這麼窮的>> Frances Cheng: But big corporations like Vision are the very reason why they ended up this poor. Frances Cheng: Big corps wiped out jobs with AI and automation. Frances Cheng: Otherwise these contributors could have been living comfortably, with decent jobs. Frances Cheng: Shouldn't the corporations be held liable for what they destroyed? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->But what can they do? Halt technology advancement? The world moves on. Frances Cheng: You're right. But that makes the situation more hopeless... Frances Cheng: And I feel more guilty. <<#GoTo(@芳開始提遊行的話題讓主角不開心)>> ->Yes. They need to contribute more to the society. Social responsibility. Frances Cheng: What Vision does is no different from other corporations. Frances Cheng: Tax evasion. Exploiting contributors to maximize profit. <<#GoTo(@芳開始提遊行的話題讓主角不開心)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->This world needs a steady supply of flops, or else everything breaks down. Frances Cheng: Is that a reason to sacrifice the poor? Frances Cheng: Do they owe the world anything? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->They are being helped. Or else they can't make a living at all. <<#GoTo(@是萬象害這些人這麼窮的)>> ->It's voluntary. They can choose not to contribute flops. Frances Cheng: But you know as well as I do, that they don't have any other option. Frances Cheng: Corporations like Vision are monetizing on this lack of options. Frances Cheng: Like you said, this could be the necessary evil. Frances Cheng: But necessary evil is still evil. <<@芳開始提遊行的話題讓主角不開心>> Frances Cheng: ... Thought: What is on her mind...? Frances Cheng: What if I told you there's an alternative way? <<#Simple(6)>> ->I don't understand. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Remember? I told you once that people are exploring another possibility, a different kind of social system. Frances Cheng: One where every member of society has a guaranteed income to cover their basic needs. Thought: Fran did talk about it, although... Frances Cheng: I think it's worthwhile. Maybe it could stop this downward spiral for good. Frances Cheng: More importantly, someone must put this possibility on the table, so that everything else could happen. Thought: I kind of see where this is going... Frances Cheng: I know a group of people working on this initiative. They are planning a march in a few weeks... Thought: Not that march thing again! Thought: We talked about this. A march not registered and approved is illegal. The police won't take it kindly. Thought: Violence is basically guaranteed. This is way too dangerous. Frances Cheng: Would you go, if I invite you to? Thought: Now we are coming to the real point... Thought: When she said a little bit of "we time", I thought... Frances Cheng: What do you think? Thought: Definitely no, and I don't want her to put herself in danger, either. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm not going. ->I'm not going, and I don't want you to, either. Frances Cheng: W-wait! I know we did cover this issue before... Thought: So why bring it up again...? Frances Cheng: Back then you thought it's dangerous, but you didn't understand! Frances Cheng: I thought when you have seen what you saw today, maybe... <<#GoTo(@芳原來都設計好要操弄我了？)>> ->Sure. Let's go together.;<> Thought: WHAT!?;//glitch效果 Thought: Am I nuts? She can't go! It's too dangerous! Frances Cheng: Really!? Frances Cheng: That is GREAT! Frances Cheng: I never thought you'd agree! At least not so easily, the way you responded last time. Frances Cheng: I knew you'd change your mind after today! Thought: Knew? <<@芳原來都設計好要操弄我了？>> Thought: She planned this all along!? Thought: I thought she was helping me to find a job... Thought: Looks like this whole thing is staged, to change my mind. Thought: When she said it's OK even if I fail the interview, it's because she didn't really care... //強化glitch <<#Simple(10)>> ->Is that what this whole job interview thing is about? To convince me about the march? Frances Cheng: No! I, I just wanted to... Thought: She's lying. I can tell... Frances Cheng: OK. For a moment, I did think about it. I admit. Frances Cheng: But I never meant it in the beginning. This is just an afterthought... Frances Cheng: I realized later that if you see what's going on with your own eyes, maybe you will support me. Frances Cheng: There was no plan, no conspiracy. I really wanted to help you find a job. Frances Cheng: Please trust me, J. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I do trust you, Fran. Frances Cheng: Thank you. Frances Cheng: I'm sorry if you feel manipulated. I apologize. ->I have serious trust issues now. Frances Cheng: But I—! Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: You have every reason to not trust me. But I really, never— Thought: Fran... <<#Simple(6)>> ->Okay, I trust you. Frances Cheng: ...!? Frances Cheng: Thank you, after everything that I said. Frances Cheng: And... <<#GoTo(@芳道歉自己這樣會讓主角覺得被操弄)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Whether you believe me or not, I had to say it. ->I want to trust you, but it feels very, very bad. Frances Cheng: ... <<@芳道歉自己這樣會讓主角覺得被操弄>> Frances Cheng: Sorry. I never wanted you to feel manipulated. Frances Cheng: You must feel terrible now. Frances Cheng: Now I see my mistakes. This won't happen again. <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: This march means a lot to me, J. Frances Cheng: I still hope you could support me. Thought: I can't and won't make decisions for Fran. Thought: And she can't expect me to happily see her get herself into trouble. //分成前面有沒有答應 //前面有答應 <<#If($PromiseFangGoParade = true)>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I said let's go together. Frances Cheng: I know you said it, still... Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: I want to hear you say it again, if only to confirm. Frances Cheng: You never agreed with me on subjects like this, not even once. So... <<#GoTo(@主角真低有這麼不認可程芳嗎)>> ->I wouldn't stop you from doing it, although I do not approve. <<#GoTo(@我不會限制你參加，但我還是不認同。)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //前面沒有答應 <<@接不接受程芳參加遊行提問>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Go, Fran. I support you. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Thank you. Frances Cheng: You have never shown any support on subjects like this. Thought: Fran, I... Frances Cheng: Feels so unreal. <<@主角真低有這麼不認可程芳嗎>> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Was I that unsupportive before? ->Come on, I can't be that stubborn? Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: You were. Frances Cheng: Every time I talk about civil rights and social movements, you'd disapprove. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<@程芳開始抱怨主角一直不認可她好久了>> Frances Cheng: You'd try to talk me out of it. Mind our own business, don't be obsessed with big ideas, so on and so forth. Thought: She's agitated... Frances Cheng: You said someone will fix things eventually if it's bad enough. Frances Cheng: Who is this someone, if everyone counts on each other? Thought: Fran knows perfectly well that there are people who think like her out there. Thought: Let them be the sacrificial lamb. She doesn't owe anybody anything. <<#Simple(6)>> ->You know there are many people who want to change things as much as you do. Frances Cheng: I do know. But look at what you did, what you are still trying to do. Frances Cheng: You want to talk me out of it, make me stop caring. Frances Cheng: Everyone who thinks like me faces the same peer pressure. Thought: I, um... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I know you always supported me, in pretty much everything, provided no danger is involved. Frances Cheng: I also know you did that out of the love for me, as this person. Frances Cheng: That love does not extend to what I strive for. Thought: No, it's because Fran never thinks twice before diving headlong into danger... Frances Cheng: I'm not blaming you. You do not have to agree with everything I believe in. Frances Cheng: Still, I hope you could care more about what I care about... Thought: I care about you, first and foremost... Frances Cheng: I'm so tired, having to constantly explain to you, petition to you... Frances Cheng: I'm starting to have this feeling that you are not able to apprehend how much this means to me... Thought: Because you never cared to comprehend how much you mean to me!;//爆氣 <<#Simple(10)>> ->Because you never cared to comprehend how much you mean to me! Frances Cheng: J, I... <<#Simple(10)>> ->You want to help people, all the time, never stopping to care for yourself. Frances Cheng: ... <<#Simple(10)>> ->It doesn't matter, you say. It's a worthy cause, you say. Frances Cheng: ... <<#Simple(10)>> ->There are people who care about you, you know? I can't lose you! I'm terrified! Frances Cheng: I, um... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Sorry... Frances Cheng: I never thought about it that way. From my perspective, it seems you were firmly against my values. Frances Cheng: Really, I... Frances Cheng: I'm really sorry, for being so self-centered. Thought: She's crying... Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: But I won't change my mind. Thought: Fran... Frances Cheng: Even if it means putting myself in danger, I still want to do something, anything, to induce change. Frances Cheng: Even if it makes you uncomfortable, I still hold this belief. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: What do you think now, J? Thought: I can't... Thought: No matter how hard I try, I simply can't deal with the idea of Fran being in danger... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I won't stop you from being who you are. Thought: What the heck am I saying!? Thought: Although... Thought: Maybe I should have told her so, long ago... Frances Cheng: Thank you. <<#Simple(6)>> ->But I want you to promise me, that you will be responsible for your own safety, for my sake. Frances Cheng: I know. I will think twice before making big decisions. Frances Cheng: Because now I know you will be heartbroken if something happens to me. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->I won't change my mind, either. I can't watch you diving into danger. Frances Cheng: I understand... Frances Cheng: This is how relationships go, I suppose. Support each other, but not in everything. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Sorry, I simply can't back down on this. Frances Cheng: Don't be. I didn't back down, myself. Frances Cheng: The key is to recognize and respect differences... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Now I know that... Frances Cheng: ...that someone cares about me, more than I do. Frances Cheng: Thank you, J. <<#GoTo(@程芳頂樓主題嚴肅對話結束)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I wouldn't stop you from doing it, although I do not approve. <<@我不會限制你參加，但我還是不認同。>> Frances Cheng: I know you respect my opinions and my rights... Frances Cheng: Still, I wish to have your approval. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You're asking for too much, Fran. Frances Cheng: I know... Frances Cheng: It's just that... ->I can't approve you putting yourself in danger. Frances Cheng: I see... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Okay. I shouldn't be surprised. You're always like that. <<#Simple(6)>> ->What? ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@程芳開始抱怨主角一直不認可她好久了)>> ->Why do you insist on this march thing? ($WhyDoYouSoInsist) <> Frances Cheng: I agree with you on the necessary evil. Vision, flop contribution, everything. Frances Cheng: But I don't believe we must live with it forever. There could be better alternatives along the way, as long as we don't stop looking. Frances Cheng: First, make ourselves heard. From one voice to a discussion, which ultimately leads to changes for the better. Thought: Making yourselves heard also means attracting the wrong kind of attention... <<#Simple(6)>> ->But activities like this march come with risks. Frances Cheng: I know, and I accept these risks. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: So? Are you OK with me going to the march now? <<#GoTo(@接不接受程芳參加遊行提問)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->Yes. This whole business is exploitation. Frances Cheng: What makes you think so? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->The contributors deserve better. <<@玩家選因為待遇不好所以不要支持運算產業>> Frances Cheng: It would be acceptable if we paid them more? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yes, at the very least. Frances Cheng: The thing is, no matter how much cash or benefit we offer... Frances Cheng: It doesn't change the fact that they are selling chunks of their life for a living. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Doesn't that apply to all forms of jobs? Frances Cheng: You're right. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@芳開始提遊行的話題讓主角不開心)>> ->The issue is that the whole flop farming industry is built on unfairly cheap labor. Frances Cheng: Hey, you figured it out in just one day! Frances Cheng: Now you know Vision won't shoot itself in the foot, no matter how much good intention it has. Frances Cheng: Fundamentally it's a business, and a business must make profit... <<#GoTo(@芳開始提遊行的話題讓主角不開心)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->Using humans as substitutes for computers is outrageous. Frances Cheng: Do you mean the poor treatment, or objectification? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->The treatment is the primary issue. <<#GoTo(@玩家選因為待遇不好所以不要支持運算產業)>> ->The objectification of humanity is a nightmare. Frances Cheng: Right... Frances Cheng: There's always this trend, seeing workers as just "labor", which breaks down to hours, yield, and such. Frances Cheng: But what we're doing now is worse than ever before... Frances Cheng: Converting a living human, physically, into flops, with a price tag. Frances Cheng: From the executive level, it's almost impossible to realize our commodity is made from living humans. Frances Cheng: The lack of this perspective gives birth to inhumane decisions. <<#GoTo(@芳開始提遊行的話題讓主角不開心)>> ->Both. Frances Cheng: And neither seems to have a solution... Frances Cheng: Flop farming is all about scale, the number of contributors simultaneously online. Frances Cheng: If every contributor is paid a little bit more, it adds up to gigantic cost, killing the profit. Frances Cheng: And objectification is inevitable, since decision makers cannot see how this business is like a butcher shop... Frances Cheng: We convert brain power into flops, which has a definite price. Every living brain carries a price tag. Easy to manage. Frances Cheng: From the executive level, it's almost impossible to realize our commodity is made from living humans. Frances Cheng: The lack of this perspective gives birth to inhumane decisions. <<#GoTo(@芳開始提遊行的話題讓主角不開心)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->How would you define right or wrong? ($HowDoYouDefineGOrB);//選過關閉 <> Frances Cheng: If something makes the world a better place, I'd say it's right. Frances Cheng: Enable more people to take control of their lives, to live the way they choose to. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Even at the cost of stagnant economy? Frances Cheng: Of course there must be a balance between the two. Frances Cheng: Right now we have outstanding economic growth, but people don't have much quality of life. ->Economy depends on competition. Competition creates winners and losers. Frances Cheng: You're right. Frances Cheng: But fundamentally, what is the purpose of economic growth, if not to improve the quality of life for everyone? Frances Cheng: Competition and growth have become an excuse to exploit the people, for the benefit of corporations. <<#Simple(6)>> ->You're talking about communism. Frances Cheng: Not that radical, haha. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: What I meant is, the freedom of choice depends on competition, and in competition, there always are losers. Frances Cheng: Can we create a safety net, find a way to cater to the basic needs of these losers? Frances Cheng: In a way, flop farming is this safety net. It gives those who are truly desperate a last chance. Frances Cheng: Or is it the opposite? This "safety net" is a cleverly dressed up way of exploitation? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I wish to do something, to advocate for the wellbeing of everyone. Frances Cheng: But these days, sometimes I feel maybe I picked the wrong side. <<#GoTo(@程芳問她參與算力供給產業是不是對的？)>> ->I don't quite know what you mean. ($GOrBWhatAreYouTalking);//選過關閉 <> Frances Cheng: Flop farming took from contributors a substantial chunk of their life, with pathetic pay in return. Frances Cheng: At the same time, it offers them a way out. Frances Cheng: When every path looks like a dead end, we offer them a way to fast forward. Put them to sleep. Black out. Skip the miserable part. Frances Cheng: When they wake up, there's a little fortune waiting, for what's left of their life. Frances Cheng: The stability of this society hinges on this mechanism. Frances Cheng: From my perspective, I'm trying to give them more help from within the system. Frances Cheng: From a different perspective, though, am I an agent of evil who encourages them to embrace exploitation? Frances Cheng: What would you say, J? <<#GoTo(@程芳問她參與算力供給產業是不是對的？)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<@程芳頂樓主題嚴肅對話結束>> <<#End>>

title: 第一天晚上在主角腦海裡
body: //主角評價玩家的操作，覺得主角 沒什麼問題 做得好 //可評價事件1禮物2答應程芳或接受她去遊行3搞砸面談(阿姨抱氣)4搞砸工作或是感覺幫她拿到工作(抱怨阿姨玩家能忍受真厲害)5打持刀瘋子 Jason Dai: You, again. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Jason Dai? Jason Dai: Who else could it be? ->Where are we? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: So, you really have no idea, do you? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Right. What's happening? What is this place? Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: We are inside my mind, probably. Jason Dai: You will be trapped here for a while, until my body wakes up. ->I guess I have sort of figured it out. Jason Dai: ...I see. Jason Dai: Welcome, then, to my subconsciousness, or whatever. Jason Dai: Mind you. This happens only when my body falls asleep. Jason Dai: You can leave when it wakes up. <<#EndSimple>> <<#Simple(6)>> ->How do you know?;<> Jason Dai: Out of experience. ->You have seen things like this before?;<> Jason Dai: You're a sharp one. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: I can tell you are different from the others, though. Why? <<@回答是杰自己為何不同>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What do you mean by "different"? ($MCAskingWhyGlitch);<> Jason Dai: All this fuzzy...noise, around you. Jason Dai: I've never seen anything like this before. What made you special? <<#GoTo(@回答是杰自己為何不同)>> ->"The others"? ($MCAskingWhyGlitch);<> Jason Dai: Yeah, those who came before you. <<#If($AskMCHappenedBefore=1)>>;//上面有問。 Jason Dai: I did say you're not the first. <<#EndIf>> <<#If($AskMCHappenedBefore=2)>>;//上面沒問 <<#Simple(6)>> ->You've been controlled by others before? Jason Dai: Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: Hey, answer me. Why aren't you like the others? <<#GoTo(@回答是杰自己為何不同)>> ->Probably because I was sent here against my will. Jason Dai: "Here", you mean my mind. <<#If($AskMCHappenedBefore=2)>> <<#Simple(6)>> ->You're sharp, aren't you?;//有走敏銳路線 Jason Dai: Is that supposed to be sarcastic? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No. Jason Dai: ... ->I was trying to lighten you up. Jason Dai: Acknowledged. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Um, sorry? Jason Dai: What for? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Aren't you mad? ->Thought you'd like to hear me say sorry. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Not mad at all, and don't be sorry. Jason Dai: I'm just saying what I see out loud. ->Are you always so serious? ->Boring. Jason Dai: I'm only trying to sort things out. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Sent against your will, you said? <<#EndIf>> ->Not 100% sure, but I wouldn't want to be here, if given the choice. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Meaning you didn't mean to occupy my body? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yes. Jason Dai: I see. ->What does it matter to you? Jason Dai: No, nothing. It's just that I saw some possibilities. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Can I know a bit more? I mean, about you? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Like, who I am? Jason Dai: That's a good start. ->What do you want to know? <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Starting from your name, if you have one? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->"M.B.U." Jason Dai: That... Jason Dai: That doesn't sound like a name. A codename, maybe? ->I don't know. Jason Dai: Figured. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: What do you remember, before being sent into my body? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I was trapped inside another person, a man. Jason Dai: Trapped? Not your own choice? ->I had to follow orders, from some strange people. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: In other words, you were controlled by someone, maybe an organization. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: How did you barge into my mind? Jason Dai: Also following their orders? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No. They were killed before I was sent here. <> Jason Dai: ...What? Jason Dai: Killed? By whom? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I really don't know. ->It was something like a specter. <<#EndSimple>> ->Something seriously weird sent me over. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: We're only getting more questions than answers here. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: To sum it up, you were dumped into a stranger's body, totally unprepared, and stayed for a whole day. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah. I improvise. Jason Dai: Is that so? ->I pretend this is a video game. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: This video game is my life. <<#EndSimple>> //讓阿姨爆氣 <<#If($InterviewHandleBadCount>1)>> Jason Dai: You really pissed Ms. Yeh off today. The consequences are all on me. //阿偉喜歡主角 <<#If($EndingChungWeiScoreCoLin=5)>> Jason Dai: That Wayne Zheng likes you quite a bit, though. Jason Dai: Hmmm... Jason Dai: Or maybe I should say, "likes me"? <<#EndIf>> //阿偉不喜歡主角 <<#If($EndingChungWeiScoreCoLin<5)>> Jason Dai: And that Wayne Zheng doesn't seem to see eye to eye with you, either. Jason Dai: Hmmm... Jason Dai: Doesn't see eye to eye with me, is more like it. <<#EndIf>> <<#GoTo(@士傑哥哥抱怨玩家第一日)>> <<#EndIf>> //阿姨普通 <<#If($InterviewHandleBadCount=1)>> Jason Dai: ...Although you did survive the day, uneventfully. Jason Dai: That nitpicky boss of ours was reasonably cool about you. <<#GoTo(@一起抱怨阿姨時間)>> <<#EndIf>> //阿姨喜歡，阿姨普通就不進來 <<#If($InterviewHandleBadCount=0)>> Jason Dai: That nitpicky Ms. Yeh does like you, though. <<@一起抱怨阿姨時間>> <<#Simple(6)>> ->You don't like her, either? Jason Dai: What's with that "either"? You don't like her? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I like her, sort of. ->Don't really have any personal opinion. Jason Dai: Is that so? ->I don't like her. Jason Dai: I can relate. People like her don't tend to make friends. <<#Simple(6)>> ->What kind of people are you referring to? Jason Dai: That is off the subject. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sorry. Let's stay focused. Jason Dai: Hmm... ->You wandered off first. Jason Dai: You're right. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Obsessive, control freak, manipulative, that kind. ->Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> //阿緯喜歡主角 <<#If($EndingChungWeiScoreCoLin=5)>> Jason Dai: Say, the Screening lead likes you, too, I think... Jason Dai: Hmmm... Jason Dai: Or maybe I should say, "likes me"? <<#EndIf>> <<#EndIf>> <<@世傑哥哥感謝沒搞砸第一日>> Jason Dai: Everything considered, thank you for not screwing up my day. You handled everything well. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Don't mention it, bro! Jason Dai: Yeah. ->Um, thank you for thanking me? Jason Dai: You do owe me a thank you, using my body without consent. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I didn't mean to. Jason Dai: So you say. I'm not ruling out other possibilities. ->You're right. In that case, I owe you a sorry, too... Jason Dai: Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->I just followed my heart. Jason Dai: Keep following it, then, if not too much of a trouble. <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@跳過主角第一日評價玩家時間)>> <<@士傑哥哥抱怨玩家第一日>> Jason Dai: I must take back my body asap, before you do any more damage. <<#Simple(12)>> ->You don't like what I did today? Jason Dai: Yep. <<#Simple(6)>> ->And? Jason Dai: And no more expectations. I see this is way beyond your capability. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You're a dick, you know that? Jason Dai: This dick is giving an objective statement. Jason Dai: People do call me a dick, Fran included. ->Thank you for your understanding. Sarcasm intended. Jason Dai: You're welcome. <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->I...apologize? Jason Dai: No need. You don't owe me anything. ->Why did you expect me to do you any favor, to begin with? Jason Dai: You're right. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<@跳過主角第一日評價玩家時間>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Anyway... Jason Dai: We must figure out a way to get you out of my body. Jason Dai: I don't think you will stay for long, although... <<#Simple(8)>> ->I wouldn't be so optimistic. Jason Dai: Is that so? ->Not for long? How do you know? Jason Dai: From experience. No one has stayed inside me for over a week yet. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: If you have no say in this matter, you'll have to live my life in my stead. Jason Dai: Please try to be considerate. I don't want to face one heck of a mess when you leave. //如果剛剛好評價 <<#If($InterviewHandleBadCount<2)>> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Huh? Didn't you say I'm doing pretty well? Jason Dai: Ah, I did... Jason Dai: Let me put it another way. Jason Dai: I don't want to think about the possibility of facing one heck of a mess when you leave. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Can you try to be, like, nicer...? Jason Dai: Hmph, sorry... Jason Dai: Didn't mean to be rude. Jason Dai: I'm not very good at dealing with people. Sorry. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Apology accepted. Haha! Jason Dai: Thank you. ->You won't make any friend, talking like that. Jason Dai: True. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: So... ->Show some encouragement, man! I can keep up the good work. Jason Dai: Could be. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: Would you like to help me? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sure, but how? <<@okok願意幫你但怎幫哩>> Jason Dai: If there are any major events upcoming, I'll give you a reminder the night before. Jason Dai: Make sure you follow my instructions. Don't try anything against my interest. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: ... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Huh? ->What's with the long pause? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: The most important of all, to me, is Fran. Jason Dai: Keep her out of harm's way at all costs. Everything else is dispensable, myself included. <<#Simple(6)>> ->You think she's in danger? Jason Dai: She's safe enough, although... ->Of course. Don't worry. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: If she attempts to dive into something silly, keep her out of it. Will you? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'll try. <<@答應傑傑會攔住成芳>> <> Jason Dai: Thank you. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Ugh... Jason Dai: You're a nice guy. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You could have done without that "ugh". Jason Dai: I'm supposed to say something nice when someone does me a favor. Jason Dai: Fran reminds me all the time, although I'm still not very good at telling the occasion. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You need people to teach you manners? Jason Dai: I'm more a doer than a talker. Jason Dai: To me, one thank you goes a long way. The rest is all about proving it with action. Jason Dai: Fran doesn't agree, though. Says it's rude. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Rude indeed. Jason Dai: You think so, too. Huh... ->Not rude. It's a legit way of life! Jason Dai: I believe so. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->She's right. Listen to her. Jason Dai: She's always right. Jason Dai: I like her. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Yeah, yeah. ->She's not bad. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@答應幫助戴士傑後清醒前對話)>> ->Define "something silly". Jason Dai: Anything that might land her in danger. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Like bungee jumping? ->Could you elaborate a bit more? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: I mean her work. She's always going out of her way to set things "right". Jason Dai: The stress. The enemies she could make. Jason Dai: We just had a dispute last week, about this march she wants to take part in. <<#Simple(8)>> ->What kind of march? Jason Dai: Not 100% sure, but a march not approved by the government is illegal. Jason Dai: An illegal march means danger. ->You tried to talk her out of it, then you guys got into a fight? Jason Dai: Sort of. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: The march is not the point. We did reach an agreement. She said she won't go. Jason Dai: I'm more worried about her job. Jason Dai: Vision is not like Growell, which is a mafia pretending to be a corporation... Jason Dai: Based on what I've learned in the army, though, Vision is probably worse. Jason Dai: I can tell that Fran is plotting something. Exactly what? I have no idea. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Plotting something? ->How did you tell? Jason Dai: She's been staying up late processing paperwork unrelated to her job. Jason Dai: I asked what it was about, but her response was dodgy. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: My guess is that she wants to present a proposal to the senior executives at Vision that they won't like. Jason Dai: If things start to go south, stop her, will you? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'll try my best. <<#GoTo(@答應傑傑會攔住成芳)>> ->I don't want to influence her own decisions. Jason Dai: I'm not asking you to stop her by force. Be diplomatic. Convince her to give up, or at least think twice. Jason Dai: In that case, would you help me? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Depends, but I'll try. <<#GoTo(@答應傑傑會攔住成芳)>> ->I don't want to restrict her by any means.;<> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I see your point. Jason Dai: Hopefully it's just me worrying too much. <<#GoTo(@答應幫助戴士傑後清醒前對話)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->Gimme a reason to help you? Jason Dai: For taking over my life against my will. Jason Dai: I think this is a good enough reason? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->OK, you win. Jason Dai: Thank you. <<#GoTo(@okok願意幫你但怎幫哩)>> ->This is against my will, too. Jason Dai: I know, and I'm not judging. Jason Dai: It's just that... Jason Dai: This life is ultimately mine, and I currently have no control over it. Jason Dai: Please help me look after it. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->OK. I'll try my best. Jason Dai: Thank you. <<#GoTo(@okok願意幫你但怎幫哩)>> ->I will help you where I can, as long as it doesn't compromise my interest. Jason Dai: A good enough promise. If I'd been in your shoes, I'd do the same. <<#GoTo(@okok願意幫你但怎幫哩)>> ->Sorry, but I'd rather follow my own heart. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Is that so? Jason Dai: You're like the others. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sorry for disappointing. <<@沒有要幫世傑的意思欸>> Jason Dai: What was I thinking... Jason Dai: For a second I thought someone, no, something like you would help me out. <<#GoTo(@拒絕幫助戴士傑)>> ->Do you expect me to follow orders just like that? Jason Dai: I'm not giving orders. Sorry if you had the wrong impression. Jason Dai: I'm hoping that you could try to help me, considering you're living my life. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->"Try to" is vague. Jason Dai: Use your discretion. Within your capability. <<#Simple(12)>> ->OK. I will try to help you. Jason Dai: Thank you. <<#GoTo(@okok願意幫你但怎幫哩)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: If you're arguing about the definition of trivial phrases, I think the situation is pretty clear. Jason Dai: All I wanted to know is whether you're friendly. Jason Dai: Looks like I hoped for too much. Jason Dai: Not a big deal. I wanted to find out, now I've found out. ->What about me, living a life that I never asked for? Jason Dai: I believe our interests are aligned, so far. Jason Dai: If any conflict of interests happens among us, I can do nothing about it. Jason Dai: You control the body. You make the decision at the end of the day. Jason Dai: All I want is a verbal promise, a token of goodwill. Jason Dai: What would you say? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I won't make no promise. <<#GoTo(@沒有要幫世傑的意思欸)>> ->OK. I'll help you. Jason Dai: Thank you. <<#GoTo(@okok願意幫你但怎幫哩)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<@拒絕幫助戴士傑>> Jason Dai: That's it, then. Jason Dai: Going forward, I'll sit in the back row, watching everything you do. Jason Dai: Like before. Jason Dai: ...My body is waking up. Finally. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Wait! I have more questions. Jason Dai: Ask. No answer guaranteed. <<@第一次戴士傑內心世界問答時間不爽版>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Other people took control of you before. What did they do? ($WhenCotroledWhatYouDid);//問過消失 <> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Don't remember. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You're lying. Jason Dai: You're entitled to your opinions. ->Okay. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@第一次戴士傑內心世界問答時間不爽版)>> ->Other people took control of you before. What are they like? ($WhatDoesItLookLikeTheController);//問過消失 <> Jason Dai: Not people. Jason Dai: Other things. Jason Dai: I don't know what they are, but they are not people. The same applies to you. Jason Dai: As to what they are like... Jason Dai: Can't find any word for it. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Try to describe, as best as you can. Jason Dai: I can't. ->You're useless. Jason Dai: Given the situation... Jason Dai: Yeah, I'm useless, totally. Can't raise a finger against you and your pals. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@第一次戴士傑內心世界問答時間不爽版)>> ->I'm done with questions. Jason Dai: Whatever. <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Hmmm...? Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: It's beginning, my awareness is slipping... Jason Dai: See you tonight. //畫面開始轉場，接到第二日。 <> <<#End>> <<@答應幫助戴士傑後清醒前對話>> Jason Dai: Hmmm... Jason Dai: My body is waking up. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You can feel it? Jason Dai: Yeah. Jason Dai: Just a vague feeling. ->Time to say goodbye? Jason Dai: We have a little bit more time, I think. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: While we are still here, any more questions? <<@問戴士傑被控制的各種問題>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Other people took control of you before. What did they do? ($WhenCotroledWhatYouDid);//選過消除 <> Jason Dai: You really have no idea? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Then you must be pretty disoriented right now. ->You still think I'm pretending? Jason Dai: No, not anymore. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Meaning you did suspect me before? Jason Dai: Yes. I thought this could be an act to let my guard down, then... Jason Dai: Then I saw the problem – whatever you plan to do next, I can't do anything about it. Jason Dai: Conclusion, you don't need to pretend anything. QED. <<#Simple(6)>> ->I could be trying to squeeze information out of you. Jason Dai: You have done it before? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->That was the order they gave me in my previous body. ->Could be. Jason Dai: ... ->No, I haven't. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Roger. Jason Dai: I don't know anything of value. Jason Dai: Again, if you were trying to do that, why are you reminding me of it? ->You're right. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Great. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I don't want to go into details, about what they made me do. Jason Dai: Let's just say, quick jobs, not the good kind. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Do you know why they pick you? Jason Dai: No. Jason Dai: For being in the right place at the right time, maybe? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Anything else you want to know? <<#GoTo(@問戴士傑被控制的各種問題)>> ->Are you aware of everything that happened under my control? ($DoYouRememberWhenIControlledYou);//選過消除 <> Jason Dai: Crystal clear. Jason Dai: It feels funny. Jason Dai: As if I'm taking control of my own body, making decisions out of my own will, with no external influence at all. Jason Dai: Even if you do something that's entirely out of my character... Jason Dai: My brain will automatically find some way to legitimize it. Jason Dai: Except for situations like this, of course. <<#Simple(6)>> ->"This" means? Jason Dai: When my body falls asleep. ->Meaning sleep time? Jason Dai: Yes. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: I gain full awareness during sleep. Jason Dai: Everything rushes back in. Suddenly I can tell I was manipulated. Jason Dai: It feels... Jason Dai: Nauseating. <<#Simple(6)>> ->I'm sorry. Jason Dai: It's alright. Out of your control. ->I didn't want it to be this way. Jason Dai: I know. I don't, either. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: It will keep happening until you're out of my brain, for good. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Hold on, those thought bubbles I feel when I'm steering your body, they are...? Jason Dai: Thought bubbles? Jason Dai: I, um... Jason Dai: I'm surprised that you can feel anything at all. Jason Dai: What exactly did you feel? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sometimes I can see your thoughts. ->Your reaction to events, every now and then. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Did I show any signs of struggle against your control? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Can't say for sure. ->So far, no. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Hmmm... Jason Dai: I suppose it's just reflex. Jason Dai: Like I said, I don't have real awareness until we are sitting here. Jason Dai: Those thought bubbles do not represent my rational thoughts. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I'm confused. Jason Dai: It's alright. You don't need to figure everything out. ->Understood. Jason Dai: You've got some quick mind. Gotta give you that. Jason Dai: Even I am not entirely sure about what I just said. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Anything else? <<#GoTo(@問戴士傑被控制的各種問題)>> ->Can you open that door over there? ($CanYouOpenThatDoor);//問過消失 <> Jason Dai: This one? Jason Dai: No can do. Shove or kick, won't budge. Jason Dai: I guess there's nothing behind it. <<#GoTo(@問戴士傑被控制的各種問題)>> ->I don't have any more questions. Jason Dai: Is that so? <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: ... <<#Simple(6)>> ->What? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: I can feel that my body is already awake. It's moving around. <<#Simple(10)>> ->But who's in control? We're both here. Jason Dai: An incomplete version of me, I guess. <<#Simple(10)>> ->What? Jason Dai: My theory is that I am the real Jason Dai only when I'm here. Jason Dai: In this state, I have memory of everything I did under control, and am fully aware of being controlled. Jason Dai: There is another me, another ego, without full memory or awareness. Jason Dai: When you're not in the cockpit, it takes over. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Two versions of you in the same body? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: It's a hypothesis. Jason Dai: All memories of being controlled are locked inside this room. Jason Dai: So when you, or the others, leave, I won't feel anything out of the ordinary. <<#Simple(8)>> ->How do you know you are the real Jason Dai? Jason Dai: Because I have both sets of memories, under control and otherwise. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: But again, you can argue that I'm a fake, because I've never fully controlled this body. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Or maybe there's only one single Jason Dai all along...? Jason Dai: Can't deal with these existential questions. Let's call it a day. ->I think I know what you mean... Jason Dai: Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->I see what you mean. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: It's about time... Jason Dai: Time for you to leave. I can feel it. Jason Dai: ...;//glitch Jason Dai: I'm leaving the rest to you. Please. <<#End>>

Thought: Very much surprised.
Wayne Zheng: We are trying out this new recruitment model, and you are the very first test subject.
Wayne Zheng: According to Ms. Yeh, it should streamline the employee onboarding process, having hands-on experience on both sides.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站插腰抬頭) >>
Wayne Zheng: We work very closely with Ops.
Quentin Qiu: Very close? For real?;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站插腰抬頭返回) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看內) >>
Wayne Zheng: Hey, QQ...;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站插腰看內) >>
Quentin Qiu: They don't want to work with us. Against us is more like it.;<< Play (邱奕宏, MBU_邱奕宏_側坐後躺) >>
Quentin Qiu: Tell you what, Jayster.
Thought: Jayster?
Quentin Qiu: Consider yourself lucky, getting a job at Screening. Those people at Ops are—
Wayne Zheng: Hey, stop it.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站插腰看內返回) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看內返回) >>
<<#Simple(6)>>
->I see Screening and Ops don't get along too well.
    Wayne Zheng: ...Right.
    Wayne Zheng: We have conflicts of values every now and then.
    Wayne Zheng: But let's leave it for another day.
    Wayne Zheng: You're scheduled for field practice at Ops tomorrow. I don't want to give you wrong ideas.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Wayne Zheng: About Ms. Yeh's idea of double field practice, I have my own guesses.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站插腰抬手) >>
Wayne Zheng: I suppose she really is trying to bridge the gap between our divisions...;<< Play (邱奕宏, MBU_邱奕宏_側坐後躺返回) >>
Wayne Zheng: From the company's perspective, business divisions should be insulated from each other as much as possible, for confidentiality concerns.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站插腰抬手返回) >>
<<#Simple(4)>>
->Why's that?
    Wayne Zheng: Um, hard to explain...
    Wayne Zheng: Without any background knowledge, it could be overwhelming. You will know after working here a while.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
 
 
Wayne Zheng: Oh, did you happen to read the profiles while waiting for me?
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->I went through all of them.
    <<Set($ToldAWeiYouHadReadIt,true)>>;//進行過猜誰適配度滿份的對話
    Wayne Zheng: Fantastic!
    Wayne Zheng: You're very efficient.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站插腰點頭) >>
    Wayne Zheng: I have gone through the profiles, too. Today's consultation is going to be very easy!
    Wayne Zheng: There's a perfect candidate with 10/10 score. You know who I'm talking about?
    Thought: I can't afford to get this wrong...
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Hank Liu.
    ->Yvonne Chiang.
    ->Eugene Dai.
        Wayne Zheng: Hmm? I don't think that's the correct answer.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站插腰思考) >>
        Wayne Zheng: It's Ms. Sydney Tung.
        Wayne Zheng: A bit extra preparation won't hurt, Jason!;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站插腰思考返回) >>
        Thought: Hopefully I didn't make too much of a fool of myself...
    ->Sydney Tung.
        Wayne Zheng: Bingo!;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站插腰手指) >>
        Wayne Zheng: You have done your homework very well. Great!
        Thought: Yay! One more point for me!;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站插腰手指返回) >>
    <<#EndSimple>>
->I haven't read all of them yet.
    Wayne Zheng: I see. Take a bit more time and go through them all, please.
    Thought: He seems a bit surprised. Is he expecting me to read them all?
    Thought: Hopefully my response didn't leave him with any negative impression...
<<#EndSimple>>
Wayne Zheng: When you're ready for consultation, find me at my desk.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站插腰返回) >>
Wayne Zheng: I'll take you to the consultation lobby.
Wayne Zheng: We have some time to spare. Take the opportunity to make acquaintance with everyone.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站抬手) >>
Wayne Zheng: They might offer something helpful for your upcoming sessions.
Wayne Zheng: That's it, then.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站抬手返回) >>
<<#End>>
 
 
 
//以下沒東西
//以下沒東西
//以下沒東西
//以下沒東西

title: 小峰面談中場對話2
body: George Liao: ... George Liao: Jeff.;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_側站看外) >> George Liao: Chatting up the newbie, huh? Jeff Wu: Um, yes?;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_看外轉身) >> Jeff Wu: Ah, Mr. Liao! Wasn't expecting to see you here at this time. George Liao: I go where I go. You mind your own work. George Liao: I see you have nothing better to do?;<< Play (廖吉興, MBU_廖吉興_側站抱胸) >> George Liao: Come help me with some files. Jeff Wu: Eh? Jeff Wu: I'd better not, Mr. Liao.;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_轉身嘆氣) >> Jeff Wu: He's about to finish out. I should wait here for my turn. Jeff Wu: Just a couple of minutes. Let me take a break, will you?;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_轉身嘆氣抬手) >> George Liao: After the break you just took? Jeff Wu: W-what are you talking about?;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_轉身嘆氣抬手返回) >> George Liao: You dumped your work on Lawrence and went to take a break. Am I wrong? Jeff Wu: No such thing! Jeff Wu: I totally didn't go smoking. George Liao: Did I mention smoking? Jeff Wu: Ugh... Jeff Wu: I was out there, helping someone out!;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_轉身嘆氣轉頭) >> <<#If($TalkToLilMoutainFirst = true)>> Jeff Wu: Jason saw me leave. Ask him! George Liao: Is he lying, Jason Dai? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->He was helping someone out, yeah. Jeff Wu: Hear that?;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_轉身嘆氣轉頭返回) >> George Liao: I'm asking you again, Jason Dai. George Liao: Is he lying?;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_轉身嘆氣轉頭) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->He's telling the truth. George Liao: ...;<< Play (廖吉興, MBU_廖吉興_側站抱胸返回) >> George Liao: Okay, I trust you.;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_轉身嘆氣轉頭返回) >> George Liao: Go take your break.;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_轉身嘆氣返回) >> <> Jeff Wu: Yay! Jeff Wu: Thanks, Jason! Jeff Wu: Thank you for saving my ass. I owe you one!;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_看外轉身返回) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐看外返回) >> ->OK, he's lying. Jeff Wu: Oi! Jeff Wu: Traitor! George Liao: Good. I like honest young men. George Liao: What do you say, Jeff? <<#GoTo(@不是啊，我事情本來就完成了。)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->Honestly, he's cutting some slack. Jeff Wu: Oi! Jeff Wu: Traitor! <<@不是啊，我事情本來就完成了。>> Jeff Wu: No! I've finished my work!;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_轉身嘆氣轉頭返回) >> Jeff Wu: For the extra part, you dumped on me and I dumped on Lawrence. Fair deal! George Liao: Whatever I dumped on you is called an assignment. You do what you're assigned.;<< Play (廖吉興, MBU_廖吉興_側站抱胸返回) >> George Liao: Get moving. Or else I won't let you switch shifts next time.;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_轉身嘆氣返回) >> <> Jeff Wu: Yes, sir... Jeff Wu: Piece of advice, Jason. If you do get hired, stay away from Mr. Liao.;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_看外轉身返回) >> Jeff Wu: He's like that. Bossy, all the time. Jeff Wu: Good luck and see you around!;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_面談側坐看外返回) >> <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>> <<#EndIf>> George Liao: Shut up. <<#GoTo(@不是啊，我事情本來就完成了。)>>

title: 工作人員逃難進簽約室2
body:

Wayne Zheng: What's going on?;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_側站微慌) >>
 
Jade Lin: There's a man—
Jade Lin: —with a knife, stabbing everyone! I-I—

<<#End>>

title: 被鄭緯叫醒2
body: Wayne Zheng: ...You said Tyler got the time wrong, Lawrence? Wayne Zheng: He has got this procrastination problem, but never missed a deadline...;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐右看) >> Wayne Zheng: Did he say exactly why he's late? Lawrence Chang: No, just that he got the time wrong.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約正坐右看轉左) >> Wayne Zheng: Okay.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_簽約正坐左看返回) >>;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐右看返回) >> <<#If($HelmetSaidYoungGood = -10)>> Lawrence Chang: Hey, Jason... Lawrence Chang: There's something I must tell you.;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐認真) >> Thought: ...Why so serious, all of a sudden? Lawrence Chang: I'm so, so sorry. Lawrence Chang: I gave you the wrong advice, maybe even screwed up your decisions... Lawrence Chang: I mean, about younger candidates being better and all. Thought: Ah, that is what he means... Lawrence Chang: I didn't know it's wrong until Auntie called me to her office. Lawrence Chang: I apologize for my mistake. Lawrence Chang: I owe you one. Will make it up to you later. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Not a big deal. No worries. Lawrence Chang: Thank you for saying that, but I still owe you one!;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐認真點頭) >> Lawrence Chang: Any time you want a favor, just come and ask. ->Don't be so eager to give advice next time. Lawrence Chang: I'm so sorry, Jason.;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐認真低頭) >> Lawrence Chang: What I told you, I do believe it myself. Lawrence Chang: I'm shocked to find out it's wrong. Worse still, it affected your performance... Lawrence Chang: Let me make it up to you. I owe you one. Lawrence Chang: If you have any need that I can help, just come and ask. OK?;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐認真低頭返回) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You owe me just one? Lawrence Chang: I'm serious, Jason. Lawrence Chang: I don't usually make promises. When I say I owe you one, I intend to make it count. Lawrence Chang: Trust me.;<< PlayUntil (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐認真點頭) >> ->Oh. Thought: He's kinda disappointed. Lawrence Chang: ... Thought: I don't need to be so cold... Lawrence Chang: I'll take it as apology accepted. Thank you, Jason.;<< PlayUntil (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐認真點頭) >> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: That's it for now. Lawrence Chang: Sorry, Wayne, for killing the mood. Wayne Zheng: It's not a problem at all.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約正坐左看轉右) >> Wayne Zheng: I have no idea what happened. If you guys are good, I'm good, too.;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐認真返回) >> <<#EndIf>> <<#If($HelmetSaidYoungGood = -20)>> Lawrence Chang: Hey, Jason... Lawrence Chang: I talked to Auntie about it.;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐認真) >> Lawrence Chang: She said she won't give you negative review because of my bad advice. Thought: I didn't expect him to really do it. Lawrence Chang: It doesn't mean she will let you pass, but at least undoes some damage.;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐認真低頭) >> Lawrence Chang: Would you forgive me now? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Relax. I didn't blame you to begin with. Lawrence Chang: Really? Great!;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐認真低頭返回) >> Lawrence Chang: You're a nice guy, Jason.;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐認真點頭) >> Thought: He's genuinely happy. ->I'm not very inclined to do that. Thought: He seems a bit dejected...;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐認真低頭返回) >> ->Do I get some form of compensation? <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Alright! Lawrence Chang: I, Lawrence Chang, officially owe you one.;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐認真手指) >> Lawrence Chang: If you ever need a favor, just come and ask. <<#Simple(6)>> ->You don't need to be so formal. Lawrence Chang: Of course I should! ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: We have a deal, then.;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐認真手指返回) >> Thought: He doesn't really listen to me... Wayne Zheng: ... Lawrence Chang: Sorry, Wayne, for killing the mood. Wayne Zheng: It's not a problem at all.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約正坐左看轉右) >> Wayne Zheng: I have no idea what happened. If you guys are good, I'm good, too.;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐認真返回) >> <<#EndIf>> Lawrence Chang: By the way, did you do it?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約正坐右看轉左) >> Wayne Zheng: ... <<#Simple(6)>> ->Do what? Lawrence Chang: That question was for Wayne. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: I haven't asked yet!;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_簽約正坐左看) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約正坐左看轉右) >> Lawrence Chang: Don't tell me you got cold feet again!?;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐傻眼) >> Lawrence Chang: Tell you what, Wayne's been wanting to ask a girl out for like, forever.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_簽約正坐左看返回) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約正坐右看轉左) >> Lawrence Chang: To help him out, we chipped in and bought him tickets to this super popular movie. Lawrence Chang: And what now? Lawrence Chang: He's too chicken to tell her! <<#If($PushChunWeiToAskHerOut,true)>> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Actually, I gave him a push, too. Lawrence Chang: Huh? And? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Guess? ->He chickened out. Lawrence Chang: For real? Wayne? Wayne Zheng: R-real what?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約正坐左看轉右) >> Lawrence Chang: Dude! You chickened out!? Wayne Zheng: I...sort of. Lawrence Chang: Geez!;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約正坐右看轉左) >> <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Wayne Zheng: OK! Enough about that!;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_簽約正坐左看) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約正坐左看轉右) >> Wayne Zheng: Now I need to go over the contract signing process with Jason. Lawrence Chang: Sure. I'll go tell everyone about Sir Wayne's courageous deed.;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐傻眼返回) >> Wayne Zheng: ...Do whatever you want. Thought: The face of a man quietly accepting his fate. Wayne Zheng: I need to check a few things with you, Jason. Wayne Zheng: There was a stack of brochures, mailed out together with your interview notice. Have you read them? Thought: Aaaand the notice explicitly said "no preparation needed"... Thought: I did read through everything in the package, though. Good for me. Thought: It was some sort of contract, pain in the butt to read... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah, I have. Wayne Zheng: Good for you!;<< PlayUntil (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_簽約正坐左看點頭) >> Wayne Zheng: You have passed the test. Thought: What does he mean? ->Why did the notice say no preparation needed, if you don't mind me asking? Wayne Zheng: Ahahaha... Thought: A guilty laugh... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Honestly... Wayne Zheng: It's imposed by Ms. Yeh. Wayne Zheng: In her own words, "we don't consider anyone who doesn't bother to read the brochures". Thought: She's going out of her way to challenge people... Wayne Zheng: I guess you wouldn't be too surprised, after everything you've seen. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->It's nothing. I don't mind. Wayne Zheng: In that case, you will get along very well with her. ->This could take a lot of getting used to. ->Gotta say, she's unfair. Wayne Zheng: A lot of job seekers would agree with you. <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: I'll trust you on your words, then. So you have read the brochures. Wayne Zheng: Or else you're already disqualified. Thought: That was a close one... Wayne Zheng: What you have read include Vision's contributor contract templates.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_簽約正坐左看返回) >> Wayne Zheng: Again, assuming you really did read them, you must have noticed there are clauses beneficial to the contributors. Thought: Where is this conversation going...? Wayne Zheng: Now let me add one more thing, about these beneficial clauses...;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_簽約正坐左看放下) >> Wayne Zheng: If they don't actively ask about them, we have no obligation to inform them. Wayne Zheng: The company does not encourage disclosing unnecessary information. Wayne Zheng: You know what I mean? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You mean don't tell them about how they can get paid more? Wayne Zheng: Simply put, yes. Wayne Zheng: Of course, if they do ask for it, we cannot decline. <<#Simple(8)>> ->This is ridiculous. Wayne Zheng: The laws require us to "make necessary benefits or subsidies available to flop contributors". Wayne Zheng: Note the wording. Make available, not actively provide. So it has become an on-demand basis. Wayne Zheng: The company, having cost control in mind, of course wouldn't want us to tell the contributors what they don't know. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Understood. ->Gotta say, this is exploitation. Wayne Zheng: Or real welfare, if you look at it the other way. Wayne Zheng: We pay contributors more than anyone else does. I'm talking about the base contract. Wayne Zheng: These additional clauses add more icing to the cake. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->I see. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->I understand. Wayne Zheng: Great. <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: I'm bringing this up, because it relates to a key performance metric at Screening. Thought: What? Wayne Zheng: The company's AI system keeps track of every team member's contract profit. Wayne Zheng: If you give contributors too much benefit, it will show on your performance review in a negative way. Wayne Zheng: This whole process is handled by Silencio, who overrides me, and even Ms. Yeh. Wayne Zheng: You should keep that in mind. Thought: He's effectively telling me not to give any benefit. Thought: Lower contract profit probably means reduced chance for this job... Wayne Zheng: Now, the specifics.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_簽約正坐左看放下返回) >> Wayne Zheng: There are three types of benefits, or losses, depending on your perspective. Wayne Zheng: One, first timer bonus. Wayne Zheng: If the candidate is contributing flops for the first time, we can increase their pay. Wayne Zheng: Two, insider bonus. Wayne Zheng: If a candidate is, or was, an employee at Vision, they are entitled to additional pay. Thought: These clauses do offer solid benefits, yeah. Wayne Zheng: Last, free insurance. Wayne Zheng: If the candidate does not have any accident insurance policy to cover the duration of flop contribution, we can offer free insurance. Thought: Huh? Insurance is extra benefit...? <<#Simple(8)>> ->I thought insurance should be part of the basic package? Wayne Zheng: In other industries, yes. Wayne Zheng: For flop farming, though, the whole business depends on cost control. Wayne Zheng: If free insurance is made part of the basic package, no farm could last two months, not a single one. Thought: That... Thought: Makes sense. ->... <<#EndSimple>> //吳晉峰走過來 <> Jeff Wu: Hey!;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約正坐右看轉左) >>;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐左看) >> Jeff Wu: Ready to come in?;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_簽約正坐左看) >> Jeff Wu: Huh? You guys are next? Jeff Wu: Your candidate is already inside. I told him about the basics. Jeff Wu: He's kinda uneasy. Dunno what for.;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐左看返回) >> Thought: He means Hank Liu? Wayne Zheng: Thank you. We will take over from here.;<< PlayUntil (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_簽約正坐左看點頭) >> Lawrence Chang: Good luck!;<< Play (張隆生, MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐右看) >> Jeff Wu: You need good luck. How many complaints have you got? Three? Lawrence Chang: Haha! I could use some good luck.;<> Jeff Wu: Put that smiley face away. Come over here. I'll give you a free lesson on contracts. Lawrence Chang: What? But I've got to... Jeff Wu: You have time! Hurry up! Lawrence Chang: Okay... Jeff Wu: I'll take this guy elsewhere. Jeff Wu: Good luck, Jason.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Thank you. Jeff Wu: No problem. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> Wayne Zheng: Let's go. <<#End>>

title: 一進辦公室遇到葉慈恩要離開2
body: Edith Yeh: We heard about it, Jason. Thought: Why is she so...grim?;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側站低頭) >> Edith Yeh: Sorry to hear it, and this is only your first day. Edith Yeh: Are you alright? Are you hurt?;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側站低頭返回) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm fine. Edith Yeh: Great. ->Well, you should see the other guy. Edith Yeh: ...Huh. Edith Yeh: I heard you took him down. Good for you. Thought: Somehow, she doesn't sound very happy about it...? <<#EndSimple>> Edith Yeh: Did Wayne say something before you left?;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側站插腰) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->He said halt everything related to contract signing for the day. Edith Yeh: Acknowledged. Edith Yeh: I will send the message out. Edith Yeh: Thank you. ->He said everyone is off work for the day. Thought: Hmm? I'm sure that's not what he said... Edith Yeh: Really? Edith Yeh: I thought he'd only suspend work related to contract signing. Edith Yeh: ... Edith Yeh: Still, thank you. I will verify with Wayne. <<#EndSimple>> Edith Yeh: I need to make a few calls. Don't forget to clock yourself out, on your computer. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Come again? Clock what? Edith Yeh: Record the time that you stop working. Normally I should do it, but today is a special case. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Edith Yeh: And... Edith Yeh: A security officer told me about it.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側站插腰低頭) >> Thought: She looks...sadder. Edith Yeh: About what that man came here for... Edith Yeh: Or maybe you know it already?;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側站插腰低頭返回) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No. Edith Yeh: The officer who apprehended him told me that... ->Do you know it? Edith Yeh: Kind of. The security officer who apprehended him mentioned something. <<#EndSimple>> Edith Yeh: He's here to take revenge.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側站插腰返回) >> Edith Yeh: His family died here at this farm, during contribution... An accident. Edith Yeh: Multiple of them, all died. Thought: ... <<#Simple(6)>> ->Why tell me this? Edith Yeh: Because I think you deserve to know what a deplorable business you are in.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側站抬手) >> Edith Yeh: You're entitled to know. Edith Yeh: Goodbye.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側站抬手返回) >> <> Thought: Why did she say that?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看內) >> Thought: Isn't she in the same business, herself? <<#End>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Edith Yeh: Goodbye. <> <<#End>>

title: 襲擊後的辦公室對話2
body: Quentin Qiu: Hey I heard you knock that guy out all by yourself?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_背站右看) >> Quentin Qiu: And he's got a knife? Tyler Cao: I got him into this...;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外低頭) >> Cathy Hsieh: Don't say that, Tyler. <<#EndSimple>> <> Jeff Wu: You guys are still here?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_背站左看轉右) >>;<< Play (邱奕宏, MBU_邱奕宏_側坐後躺看後) >> Cathy Hsieh: Jeff! Jeff Wu: Calm down. The medics are taking care of him.;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_側站低頭返回) >> Jeff Wu: He's in stable condition. Relax. Quentin Qiu: Why is this happening? Cathy Hsieh: Did you see what happened, Jeff?;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_側站看內) >> Jeff Wu: ...Yeah, witnessed the whole thing. Jeff Wu: That psycho came out of a signing room. No one saw him. You couldn't have known.;<< Play (謝采欣, MBU_謝采欣_側身站好低頭) >> Jeff Wu: ...;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_側站看內返回) >> Jeff Wu: Then he pulled a knife out of nowhere, and stabbed Lawrence in the back. I-I wanted to help Lawrence but was too afraid to move...;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_側站低頭) >> Jeff Wu: Lawrence turned around in a blink and knocked his other knife – yeah, he had two – out of his hand, and hollered RUN!;<< Play (謝采欣, MBU_謝采欣_側身站好低頭返回) >> Jeff Wu: Everyone froze for a second, then started to run like hell.
